urinary-system.definition: The organs that remove waste from the body.
urinary-system.sentence: The urinary system is the filter of the bloodstream as it captures waste and releases it from the body.

translations.definition: Ats'íís biyi'di siláhígíí ats'íís ałtso choyoos'įįdígíí níláhgóó yits'ą́ąjį' kwííł'ínígíí

vertebrate.definition: An animal that has a spinal cord and a skeleton. Compare with invetebrate.
vertebrate.sentence: Humans, horses, and birds are vertebrates, but an octopus has no bones or spinal cord and is thus an invertebrate.

translations.definition: Hináanii naaldeehii, animals, bits'íísts'in dóó bitsiighąą' bíígháán bii' danít'i' ígíí

vestigial-trait.definition: Cells, tissues, or organs in a body that have no apparent function and is similar to a functioning organ or structure in an ancestor.
vestigial-trait.sentence: The wisdom teeth of our ancestors would replace any teeth that fell out or were removed.  Now, our wisdom teeth are vestigial traits because we have better mouth hygeine and dental care.

translations.definition: Hinááhts'óóz ats'íís biyi'di binaanish t'ááła'ígo hólónígíí, tissues, ats'ą́ siláii ats'íís biyi'di hólǫ́ nidi doo choo'į́į dago yits'ą́ą́dę́ę́' yigáałii bits'íís hólónígíí ła' choyooł'į́ dóó binaanish hólónígíí t'áá yił aheełt'é

virus.definition: A small parasite that lives within the cells of other organisms, cannot reproduce without the help of the cell it lives inside of, and causes disease.
virus.sentence: Hantavirus is a virus that causes serious lung disease. You can catch hantavirus from the saliva, urine, or droppings of deer mice and other rodents.

translations.definition: Hináanii doo yit'į́į dago nááná łahdę́ę́' yigáałgo ats'íís bihinááhts'óóz biyi'di hiná silį́į́', dóó áadi hinááhts'óóz yee binahjį' noosééł yileeh áádóó bits'ą́ą́dóó ąąh dahaz'ą́ haleeh

water-cycle.definition: The movement of water on, above, or below the surface of our planet.
water-cycle.sentence: The water cycle describes how water moves around the earth.  Water moves around when it rains, when rivers transport it long distances, when it evaporates from the surface of leaves, or when ocean currents move it around the globe.

translations.definition: Tó nahasdzáán bikáá'góó dóó biyi' hólǫ́  dóó hót'ááhgóó dahólǫ́ éí hoo'nááł dóó danílínígíí bił ahééhwiizhíísh

white-blood-cell.definition: Cells that are part of the body's immune system that help the body fight infection and other diseases.
white-blood-cell.sentence: White blood cells are part of the immune system. There are many different types of white blood cells circulating in your blood and each type has a specific role in fighting infections.

translations.definition: Hinááhts'óóz ats'íís yee bihodééłníi da áyósin éí ii'noot'į́į́', infection, dóó ąąh dahwiidooł'aałii yich'ą́ą́h naalnish

zoology.sentence: Ranchers use their zoology knowledge to breed sheep so their offspring have more meat or fluffier wool.

translations.definition: Hináanii naaldeehii, animals, ał'ąą át'éego dahyikahígíí haalzííd dóó naalkaahgo bíhoo'aah

zygote.definition: The cell that forms after gametes are joined.
zygote.sentence: The zygote forms once the sperm cell enters the egg cell and they become one cell.  Identical twins are a result of a zygote splitting into two cells.

translations.definition: Amá dóó azhé'é bik'eelyéí bee i'niitsąądgo

aadaatehigii.definition: Ha'át'íi da atom t'ááłá'ígo bee ályaaígíí. Atom t'ááłá'i yileehgo doo tanáó'níiłgo ha'át'íi da náádoodleeł da.

translations.definition: A pure substance that is made of only one type of atom. Elements cannot be broken down into any simpler substance.

entries.sentence: Elements are made up of a group of atoms that are all the same, just like a field of corn is made up of many individual corn plants.

aah-dahaza.definition: Ats'íís haashį́į́ yidzaago doo hazhó'ó naalnish da silį́į'go bee bihodiit'i'

translations.definition: A change in a living body that prevents it from functioning normally.

entries.sentence: When many think about diseases we first think about cancer or the flu. However, it is important to not forget about mental health diseases like depression or anxiety.

aah-dahaza-yilnih.definition: Ats'íís yihodiiłt'hii dóó yąąh dahooł'aahii idahidilnéehgo

translations.definition: The process of passing on illness or disease.

entries.sentence: One individual corn plant will produce between two and four ears of corn.

aahooniil-nayiilnani.definition: Ááhoo'nííł éí bee hahodilyaii t'áá ałtso nahasdzáán dóó yádiłhił bii' siláhígíí ahił nidaalnishgo nináháhááhígíí éí nayiił'ná hadoh dóó hak'az ádaat'éii.

translations.definition: Any non living factor that impacts an ecosystem. For example, temperature.

entries.sentence: Plant growth can be affected by abiotic factors like sunlight, water, and temperature.

ahihooneela.definition: Ha'át'éegi da haz'ą́ągi t'áá ałtsoní hasht'e dadiit'eeh dóó ha'át'íi da doo ba'át'e' hólǫ́ǫ da yileeh. Ha'át'íi da doo ałk'ijį dooldził da, ahíhoneel'ą́ haleeh.

translations.definition: A state of balance or stable situation where opposite effects cancel each other out and no changes are occuring.

entries.sentence: A see-saw is in equilibrium when both kids are perfectly balanced on it.

ahoodziil.definition: T'áádoole'é biziil yee biinéí dóó bidziilgo yee naalnish, ha'át'íi da łahgo áyooliił, doodago hadoh bits'ą́ą́dóó hólǫ́ (thermal energy). Łą́ ał'ąą át'é.
ahoodziil.literal: ’ahóodziil= energy. (you modify the a depending what you're talking about)

translations.definition: The ability to do work, produce change, or create heat.  There are many types of energy.  For example, heat energy (also known as thermal energy) is created when molecules move around quickly and randomly within a substance, which releases heat.  Chemical energy is what holds atoms of a molecule or molecules of a substance together.  When these groups of atoms or molecules are separated during a chemical reaction, chemical energy is released, often as heat.  When organisms photosynthesize or digest food, chemical reactions provide chemical energy for their growth and development.

entries.sentence: The sun is a source of heat energy because it warms the earth.  Plants convert light from the sun into chemical energy through photosynthesis.

ajootiil-taa-hooshchi-bee-joogaalii.definition: Ha'át'íi da hináanii áát'įįłígíí t'áá bíni' dóó bintsékees át'éego bił yizhchį́, doo t'áá bí yíhooł'ą́ą' da.

translations.definition: An inherited behavior by animals that is not learned

entries.sentence: Building a nest is an instinct; birds know how to build nests without being taught.

akaaz-bitoo.definition: Akááz bitoo' ats'íís yee ákonízinígíí. Ats'íís biyi'di haleeh aadóó ats'íístahgóó hoo'nááł yileeh.

translations.definition: Chemical substances that act as messenger molecules in the body. After being made in one part of the body, they travel to other parts of the body where they help control how cells and organs do their work

entries.sentence: Type II diabetes is a result of being unable to regulate blood sugar levels because the body doesn't respond to a hormone called insulin correctly.

akagi.definition: Ats'íís yee hadít'éii, organ, ats'íís bik'ésti'ígíí
akagi.literal: akágí= skin (you modify the a depending what you're talking about)

translations.definition: The outermost layer of cells of any multicellular organism

entries.sentence: Your skin is your epidermis and it protects the inside of your body from harm.

translations.definition: An organ that formes the outer protective layer of the body.

entries.sentence: Our skin protects our organs from the environment by blocking moisture, preventing heat loss, and protecting us from pathogens.   Repeated sun exposure is a major cause of skin cancer.

akagi-biyidee-akah-bizhool-bikesti.definition: Ak'ah bineestiin dóó protein naneesdizgo akágí nilį́įgo t'áádoole'é bikí daasti'

translations.definition: A network of lipids and proteins that forms the boundary between the inside of a cell and the environment that surrounds it.  Also called a cell membrane.

entries.sentence: The plasma membrane is like the "skin" of organelles like mitochondria.  It separates the inside of the organelle from the surrounding environment.

akah-bizhool.definition: Hináanii bits'ą́ą́dę́ę́' dahólónígíí doo tó bee nídí'eełii ákondi t'áá yił ahedaałt'éhígíí éí yee nídí'eeł. Bee hinááhts'ózí bik'ésti'ígíí nidahaleeh.

translations.definition: Any organic material that does not dissolve in water but will dissolve in organic materials.  They are often used to make cell membranes.

entries.sentence: Butter, lard, and oil are examples of lipids, which make up our cell membranes.

akeedoo-heesda.definition: Bik'ehgo náás oochíiłii łahgo át'é silį́į́'ígíí, allele, akéédóó naalnishgo bik'ehgo ats'íís haidiił'įįh béé'ályaago naaki hwee hólónígíí.

translations.definition: The allele that only determines the phenotype when the organism has two copies of it. Compare with dominant.

entries.sentence: You have two copies of each chromosome.  Sometimes there is a recessive allele on one chromosome and a dominant allele on the other.  The recessive allele is the one that will not influence the phenotype.

akeelyei.definition: Hináanii biką'ígíí bik'eelyéí. Dabi'áádígíí ałdó' bik'eelyéí hólǫ́, biyeezhii (egg) deiłní.

translations.definition: When a single cell divides twice to produce four cells containing half the original amount of genetic information. This is how gametes are made.

entries.sentence: Scientists are concerned that climate change could cause a mass extinction event, similar to the one that occurred after the last Ice Age.

translations.definition: The male sex cell, or gamete in animals.  Compare with egg.

entries.sentence: In many species, males pass on their DNA to their offspring through their sperm cells.

akeelyei-ahidiinahgo-iiltsaah.definition: Hináanii biką' dóó bi'áádígíí bik'eelyéí ahidiina'go bits'ą́ą́dóó biyázhí iilts'ą'ígíí.

translations.definition: The process of combinine the male sperm with the female egg to produce a cell called a zygote.

entries.sentence: Most of the living things you see around you, including all plants and animals, are eukaryotes.

akeelyei-bee-idinootsaalii.definition: Bi'áadii bik'eelyéí bihinááts'óóz biyi'di iiná bitł'óóól ałk'ídaadeesdizígíí chromosomes t'áá ałní'ígo ánéelt'e' dóó biką'ii bik'eelyéí bihinááhts'óóz biyi'di iiná bitł'óóól ałk'ídaadeesdizígíí chromosomes t'áá ałní'ígo ánéelt'e'ígíí ahidiinahgo bits'ą́ą́dóó biyázhí (zygote) bits'íís bihinááhts'óóz biyi'di iiná bitł'óól ałk'ídadeesdizígíí t'áá át'é bii' dahólǫ́.

translations.definition: A haploid reproductive cell that joins with another haploid reproductive cell of the opposite sex to form a diploid zygote.

entries.sentence: Sperm and eggs are examples of haploid cells.

akeyah.definition: Haa'í da haz'ą́ągo hináanii choyooł'į́įgo yaa hóchį'gi
akeyah.literal: would need to specify "who's land/area". Keya is land

translations.definition: Any area defended by an organism or a group of similar organisms for their own use

entries.sentence: When you boil water, the water molecules will start to move around quickly as they absorb thermal energy from the burner.

alhee-hina.definition: Hináanii doo t'ááła' nilį́į da nidi ałch'ishdę́ę́' ahee hidi'ná

translations.definition: Any longǫterm physical relationship between individuals of two different species, including mutualism and parasitism.

entries.sentence: Scientists who study taxonomy found that whales are more closely related to animals on land (including humans) than they are to fish.

anaasazi.definition: Hak'éí nát'ą́ą́' ahool'áago hamá dóó hazhé'é dabishchíníinii bits'ą́ą́dę́ę́' hojizhlį́į́'ígíí

translations.definition: One from whom an individual is descended, whether on the father's or mother's side, at any number of generations.

entries.sentence: Your grandfather, great-grandmother, and great-great grandmother are all your ancestors.

atom.definition: T'áá ha'át'ííshį́į́ bee ályaii t'ááłá'í si'ą́ągo, atom wolyé, bijéí hólǫ́, bijéí biyi'di proton biniltł'ish shábik'ehgo bii' silá éí positive deiłní dóó neutron bitsiniltł'ish bąą ádin ákondi yee óchíidii, béésh ná'iiláhá nahalingo bii' hólǫ́. Atom bijéí bii' siléí

translations.definition: The smallest component of an element having the chemical properties of the element, consisting of a nucleus containing combinations of neutrons and protons and one or more electrons

entries.sentence: All matter is made up of atoms just as a crowd is made up of people. Matter can be composed of different types of atoms, or elements, just like the people in a crowd can be men or women, old or young, and short or tall.

atom-binahaazlei.definition: Atoms bee ályaii shádáahjigo bitsiniltł'ish hoo'nááłígíí

translations.definition: The components of an atom that have a negative charge

entries.sentence: Electrons are the glue that hold atoms together when they combine to form a molecule.

atom-bitsiniltlish-sha-bikehgo-silaii.definition: Atoms yee hadit'éhígíí biniltł'ish shábik'ehgo, clockwise or consistent with the sun's path, siláhígíí bąąh hólǫ́

translations.definition: The components of an atom that have a positive charge

entries.sentence: The number of protons an atom has is its atomic number.  A nitrogen atom has 7 protons while an oxygen atom has 8 protons.

translations.definition: Atom or molecule with an electric charge

entries.sentence: A neutral atom has equal amounts of positive and negative charges, but an atom that becomes more positively or negatively charged is an ion.

atsii-bitlool-bikestii.definition: Ats'íís bitł'óól bik'ésti'í éí ak'ah bizhool dóó ch'iyáán ats'íís hasht'ééyooldlíiłii bizhool bee ályaago ats;íís bikésti'

translations.definition: Network of lipids and proteins that forms the boundary between the inside of a cell and the environment that surrounds it.  Also called a plasma membrane.

entries.sentence: The cell membrane is like the "skin" of the cell.  It separates the inside of the cell from the surrounding environment.

atsiighaa.definition: Ats'íís bii' siléí ats'íís t'áá át'é naalnishgo áyósin, ádahoot'éii t'áá ałtso yaa ákonízin dóó yénálniih, dóó bik'izhdiitáanii baa nitsídzíkees dóó háni' nilíinii yiyoołnaał
atsiighaa.literal: ghąąʼ= matter. atsii= heads. So the "matter in your head"

translations.definition: The organ in the body which controls the functions of the body, interprets and stores information, and affects intelligence and emotion.

entries.sentence: Your brain gives you the ability to think, speak, move, and remember.

atsiighaa-iighaan-biiniti.definition: Ats'íís yee hadít'éii atsiighąą' bits'ánít'i' dóó bíígháán bii' nít'i'go ats'iighąą' dóó ats'íístahgó áhoot'éii yił ééhósin

translations.definition: Structure that begins at the the brain and continues down the spine and carries messages between the brain and the rest of the body.

entries.sentence: Starch is found in corn, potatoes, rice, and pasta.  When you eat these foods, the starch is broken down into sugar.

atsiil-chooinigii.definition: Dahináanii daneeznánée dadiłdzid bits'ą́ą́dóó ha'át'íi da nihwiileehígíí biziil (energy) bits'áłdohígíí (heat) bee nida'anish. Nahasdzáán biyi'di dahólǫ́ haashį́į́ danéelt'e'go. Łeejin/tsékǫ', ak'ah łizhiní dóó níłch'i  ászólí ááh ha'ní.

translations.definition: A material formed from the decomposing organisms that can release energy as heat or be used for work. These fuels are found within the Earth and exist in limited quantities. Examples include coal, oil, and natural gas.

entries.sentence: Oil, gas, and coal are called fossil fuels because they are made from the carbon and hydrogen of living things from millions of years ago. Power plants release carbon dioxide when they burn fossil fuels, which contributes to climate change.

atsiis.definition: Hinááhts'óóz ats'íís biyi'di binaanish t'ááła'ígo hólónígíí, tissues, ats'ą́ siláii ats'íís biyi'di hólǫ́ nidi doo choo'į́į dago yits'ą́ą́dę́ę́' yigáałii bits'íís hólónígíí ła' choyooł'į́ dóó binaanish hólónígíí t'áá yił aheełt'é

translations.definition: Cells, tissues, or organs in a body that have no apparent function and is similar to a functioning organ or structure in an ancestor.

entries.sentence: The wisdom teeth of our ancestors would replace any teeth that fell out or were removed.  Now, our wisdom teeth are vestigial traits because we have better mouth hygeine and dental care.

atsiis-adikiji-naalnish.definition: Ats'íís iisiihgo ádík'ijį' naalnish yileehgo ats'íís doo bąąhtééh da yę́ę niyiiłchxǫǫh yileeh

translations.definition: Occurs when the body's immune system attacks and destroys healthy body tissue by mistake

entries.sentence: Sometimes your body's immune system mistakes your own cells as pathogens, this can cause autoimmune disease.

atsiis-alaa-ate-naashchaa.definition: Bik'ehgo náás oochíiłii ał'ąą ádaat'éego bee hazhdít'éhígíí naasch'ąą'go ál'įįhígíí. Dik'ą́ągo si'ą́ą łeh.

translations.definition: A square drawing used to show the different genotypes possible in the offspring when two individuals reproduce

entries.sentence: By drawing a punnet square, doctors can easily show new parents what blood types their children could have.

atsiis-bee-doo-bihodeelniinii.definition: Ats'íís bee doo bihodééłníinii ats'íís hasht'éé yoodlíiłii (protein) áyiił'įįh ats'íís atíidoolíłii yaa ákoniizįįhgo

translations.definition: A protein produced by the body's immune system when it detects harmful substances

entries.sentence: Antibodies are used by your body to defend itself against things like viruses, bacteria, or even cancer cells that may cause you harm.

atsiis-bee-haleehii-alchi-nanideeh.definition: Bee iiná hodooleełii nucleotide naakigo ahidii'ash éí ats'íís bitł'óól DNA yileeh

translations.definition: Two nucleotides (also known as bases) that combine and join to make DNA. Base A combines with T and base G combines with C.

entries.sentence: DNA is shaped like a spiral staircase.  Like pieces of a puzzle, only certain base pairs can fit together to make the steps of the staircase.

atsiis-bee-nihwiileehii-ba-sileii.definition: Ha'át'íi da náádaa łahdę́ę́' ha'át'íi da bił ahiih yilyéehgo bitsiniltł'ish bilááh éí doodago a'oh yileeh  taah ji'áahgo níłch'i hydrogen bitsiniltł'ish náyiilááh

translations.definition: The building block of protein. There are ą) amino acides, and each is specified by a DNA segment of three nucleotides (bases).

entries.sentence: Different kinds of amino acids create proteins of many shapes and functions, just as different kinds of building materials can be used to make anything from a castle to a school to a shed.

translations.definition: A substance that becomes more positively or negatively charged during a chemical reaction and accepts hydrogen ions when dissolved in water.

entries.sentence: Many toothpastes include a base like baking soda as an ingredient because it can prevent bacteria from growing in your mouth.

atsiis-bikoo.definition: Ats'íís bitł'óól éí ats'íís bee ályaaígíí ts'ídá t'ááłá'ígo si'ánígíí náhidínísé, binaagóó yaa ákonízin, dóó ádéé'ál'į́įgo noosééł.

translations.definition: The cell is the smallest unit with the basic properties of life, such as growth, sensing the environment, and reproduction.

entries.sentence: Your body is composed of around 37 trillion cells! That is a big number but there are even more bacteria cells that live on and in you: around 100 trillion.

atsiis-bitlool.definition: Ats'íís bitł'óól éí ats'íís bee ályaaígíí ts'ídá t'ááłá'ígo si'ánígíí náhidínísé, binaagóó yaa ákonízin, dóó ádéé'ál'į́įgo noosééł.
atsiis-bitlool.comment: refers to DNA in cell. Often used when talking about cancer

translations.definition: The cell is the smallest unit with the basic properties of life, such as growth, sensing the environment, and reproduction.

entries.sentence: Your body is composed of around 37 trillion cells! That is a big number but there are even more bacteria cells that live on and in you: around 100 trillion.

atsiis-bitlool-altsa-dahinise.definition: Ats'íís bitł'óól ałts'á níséehgo bee ats'íís bitł'óól ániidígíí náhoodleeł

translations.definition: The formation of new cells when an existing cell divides.

entries.sentence: Bacteria like E. coli reproduce by cell division, where one cell copies itself and splits into two cells.

atsiis-bitlool-altsanise.definition: Ats'íís bitł'óól ałts'á níséego iiná bitł'óól (DNA) bik'ehgo náás oochíiłii biyi'di naaki ałch'į' naazlá, éí t'áá ahedaałt'éego ałts'ákah dóó ahidiníł náádę́ę́' niikah áádóó ałts'á nísééh.

translations.definition: The stage of cell division in mitosis or meiosis in which the doubled set of chromosomes separates into two identical groups that move to opposite ends of the cell

atsiis-bitlool-biziil.definition: Ats'íís bitł'óól biyi'di t'áádoole'é si'ą́ągo ats'íís bitł'óól yee biinéí dooígíí bii' nihwiileeh dóó náásgóó ííł'į́

translations.definition: Principal molecule for storing and transferring energy in cells.

entries.sentence: Cells use the food you eat to make  adenosine triphosphate, which  stores chemical energy that can be used  to do work like move molecules or grow.

atsiis-hina-silii-naalkaah.definition: Hináanii bits'íís naalnishígíí éí doodago na'alkaahk'ehgo dahináanii bits'íís naalnishígíí bíhoo'aah.

translations.definition: How an organism's body functions or the scientific study of how organisms function

entries.sentence: You are studying physiology when you study how blood moves through the heart or nutrients move through a plant.

atsiis-silii-yiti-doo-bineelaah-biighah.word: ats'íís silį́į' yit'į́ dóó bí'neel'ąąh bíighah
atsiis-silii-yiti-doo-bineelaah-biighah.definition: Bíla'ashdla'ii hazlį́į'go ánoolnin dóó át'éegi yit'į́įgo dóó bí'neel'ąąh bihónéedzą́ągo.

translations.definition: A trait of an individual that can be observed or measured

entries.sentence: Your hair color is an example of your phenotype.

atsiis-siziligii.definition: Dahinánii dóó naaldeehii bits'íís silzil dóó náhiniik'áásígíí t'áá łáhági silkidgo ádayósinígíí. Binaadę́ę́' nahooniidoh dóó náhoniik'asígíí éí doo binahjį' bits'íís sizil da.

translations.definition: An organism whose internal temperature can be contantly maintained and does not depend on the temperature of their surrounding environment

entries.sentence: You are an endotherm! Endotherms often have hair or feathers to help keep warm and may sweat or pant to cool down. These adaptations help them maintain a constant temperature.

atsiis-yee-hinaanii.definition: T'áá ha'át'ííshį́į́ yidą́ągo bee na'nise', bee anoosééł, dóó binahjį' naanish ła' yit'ínígíí.

translations.definition: A substance that is needed for healthy growth, development, and function

entries.sentence: Foods like vegetables and meats are high in nutrients and will help you grow.  Foods like potato chips and candy provide few nutrients to help sustain your body.

atsiistahdi-ahil-naalnishi.definition: Atsą́siléí t'áá ał'ąą át'éego binaanish dahólónígíí.

translations.definition: A group of tissues in the body that has a specific form and function

entries.sentence: Your eyes are organs that help you see, while your heart is an organ that helps circuluate your blood throughout your body.

atsiistsin.definition: Ats'íís biyi'di nitł'izgo ats'íís bá naaz'á éí ats'íís yee sizį́ dóó naha'ná

translations.definition: The internal hard structure that supports the body of a living thing

entries.sentence: Without a skeleton, our body would have no shape and we would be unable to move in a coordinated way.  We would look like a pile of organs.

atsiniltlish.definition: T'áá ha'át'íi da protons dóó electrons bináhaazléí wólta'go bitsiniltł'ish ánéelt'e dóó ábóodziilígíí

translations.definition: The amount of electricity an object has, determined by the number of protons and electrons it has

entries.sentence: An element has a neutral charge if it has an equal number of protons and electrons. It has a +1 positive charge if it has one more proton than electron.  It has a -1 negative charge if it has one more electron than proton.

atsiniltlish-bilaah-ei-doodago-bioh-aboo.word: atsiniltł'ish bilááh éí doodago bi'oh ábóodziil
atsiniltlish-bilaah-ei-doodago-bioh-aboo.definition: Ha'át'íi da bitsiniltł'ish bilááh éí doodago a'oh yileehgo taah ji'áahgo níłch'i hydrogen bitsiniltł'ish hólǫ́ǫgo bits'á shígish.

translations.definition: A substance that becomes more positively or negatively charged during a chemical reaction and releases hydrogen ions when dissolved in water.

entries.sentence: An acid - like lemon juice or  vinegar - is useful in a marinade because it breaks down the tissue and allows flavor and oil to enter.

atsiniltlish-yooleelii.definition: Ats'óóz t'áá łá'í nít'i'ígíí bitsiniltł'ish bąąhgóó hólǫ́. Nerve cell ałdó' deiłní.

translations.definition: Cells that carry electrical charges through the body and are part of the nervous system. Also known as a nerve cell.

entries.sentence: Neutrons have the same weight as protons but have no charge. Together with protons, they form the nucleus of the atom and determine its weight.

atsooz.definition: Ats'óóz t'áá łá'í nít'i'ígíí ats'íístahdi łą́ągo ahidadiit'i'go ats'íís yee ákonízin

translations.definition: Nerves are a collection of neurons that carry signals throughout the body

entries.sentence: Parkinson's disease is a disorder of the nervous system that occurs when neurons start to die, which affects the body's movement

atsooz-bee-hahodite.definition: Atsiighąą', atsiighąą' íígháán bii'nít'i'ígíí dóó ats'óóz t'áá ałtso hináanii bits'íís yee hadít'éhígíí ats'íístahgóó da'ahi'niihgo ats'íís hiná.

translations.definition: The brain, spinal cord, and nerves of an animal that together receive messages from other parts of the body and control how the body functions

entries.sentence: Each of your fingertips has over 3,000 neurons which send signals to your brain about the things you touch or feel.

translations.definition: A part of a neuron that contains the nucleus where incoming signals are received.

entries.sentence: The cell body of a neuron is like a train station.  It coordinates incoming and outgoing signals for the neuron.

awee.definition: Amá dóó azhé'é bik'eelyéí bee i'niitsąądgo

translations.definition: The cell that forms after gametes are joined.

entries.sentence: The zygote forms once the sperm cell enters the egg cell and they become one cell.  Identical twins are a result of a zygote splitting into two cells.

translations.definition: The female sex cell, or gamete. Compare with sperm.

entries.sentence: In many species, females pass on their DNA to their offspring through their egg cells.

ayoonaal.definition: Ayoonaałgo bik'ehgo náás oochíiłii łahgo ánídaa'níiłgo bik'ehgo ahii' háá' ayoochííł.

translations.definition: Changes in the frequency of alleles over generations

entries.sentence: Over the course of evolution, birds like ostriches lost the ability to fly as they evolved to survive well without flight.

azazi.definition: Hak'éí nát'ą́ą́' ahool'áago hamá dóó hazhé'é dabishchíníinii bits'ą́ą́dę́ę́' hojizhlį́į́'ígíí

translations.definition: One from whom an individual is descended, whether on the father's or mother's side, at any number of generations.

entries.sentence: Your grandfather, great-grandmother, and great-great grandmother are all your ancestors.

azhool-alchi-silahigii.definition: Atom naaki éí doodago díkwíi da ahidiilyáago binitsiniltł'ish bináhaazléí (electrons) ahiidiinił

translations.definition: Two or more atoms that are joined because they share electrons

entries.sentence: Water is a molecule because it is made when two hydrogen atoms and one oxygen atom combine together.

aziil.definition: Hado bee ayoonaałgo t'áá ha'át'íi da yee hadít'éii, molecules, ahídaha'náahgo yee niidoh dóó bits'á níłdoii

translations.definition: This type of energy (also known as heat energy) is created when molecules move around quickly and randomly within a substance, which releases heat.

entries.sentence: When you boil water, the water molecules will start to move around quickly as they absorb thermal energy from the burner.

baa-haahasin.definition: Nahasdzáán bikáá' honí'ą́ą́góó ha'át'ííshį́į́ chodaa'ínígíí nihwiileehígíí doo bilááhgóó choo'į́į da

translations.definition: When a resource from the environment is not used faster than it is produced in nature.

entries.sentence: We have a symbiosis with the bacteria that live in our gut. Our stomach and intestines give these bacteria a place to live and food to eat. In turn, the bacteria help us digest our food and protect us from pathogens in our food.

bahadzidii-ayanigii.definition: Ha'át'íi da bideezla' ayóo atí'áł'į́įgo ats'íís átííł'ínígíí

translations.definition: A highly reactive substance that causes obvious damage to living tissue

entries.sentence: Many household cleaners like bleach are corrosive because they can start to burn and harm your skin when left on the surface for too long.

bee-adiiltlaadii.definition: Bee adiiltłáadii t'áadoole'é ahidiilyéego bee bina'anishígíí

translations.definition: Any substance that increases the rate of a reaction without itself being consumed

entries.sentence: A catalyst makes an unlikely reaction more likely. For example, lactase is an enzyme catalyst that increases the rate that lactose, found in dairy products, is turned into two types of sugar that our bodies can use.

bee-atsiis-haleehii-alaaji-naalnish.definition: Bik'ehgo náás oochíiłii bee ázhnoolnin dóó ájít'éhígíí áyiił'įįh hamá dóó hazhé'é bits'ą́ą́dę́ę́' bik'ehgo náás ooch'íiłii bee hojileehígíí aheełt'éego (homozygote) éí doodago doo aheełt'ée dago (heterozygote)

translations.definition: The allele that determines the phenotype when present in a homozygote or heterozygote. Compare with recessive.

entries.sentence: An individual with one allele for type-O blood and one allele for type-B blood will have type-B blood because the  allele for type-B blood is dominant to the allele for type-O blood.

bee-atsiis-haleehii-alaaji-naalnish-bee-.word: bee ats'íís haleehii alą́ąjį' naalnish bee ájít'é yileehii
bee-atsiis-haleehii-alaaji-naalnish-bee-.definition: Ho'dizhchíídóó hojizhlį́į'go ázhnoolnin dóó ájít'éhígíí bee jinooséełii hamá dóó hazhé'é bits'ą́ą́dę́ę́' bik'ehgo náás oochíiłii alą́ąjį' (dominant) naalnishígíí bik'ehgo jinoosééł silį́į'go.  Recessive éí bik'ehgo náás oochíiłii akéédóó bik'ehgo anoosééł.

translations.definition: An inherited characteristic that appears in an offspring if it is received from a parent through a dominant allele. Compare with recessive trait.

entries.sentence: A dominant trait is determined by a dominant allele. For example, if you have an allele for light eyes and an allele for dark eyes you will have dark eyes because that allele is dominant over the allele for light eyes.

bee-doo-ihodeelniinii.definition: Hinááhts'ózí dóó akááz éí doodago atsą́ siléí bitoo' t'áádoole'é ba'át'e' dahólóonii yich'ą́ą́h naalnishígíí

translations.definition: A collection of cells and organs that help to defend an organism against pathogens.

entries.sentence: Someone who has the flu is infectious for the first couple of days because they can give the flu to others.

bee-eedahozinii.definition: Ha'át'íida t'áá aaníí át'é bee bééhózinígíí nídiilyáago binahjį' baa ha'oodzíí' dóó woodlánígíí t'áá aaníigo baa nitsáhákees.

translations.definition: The available facts or information that indicate a belief or statement is true or valid

bee-eehoznii-yeeltaii.definition: T'áá ha'át'íi da dayéélta', bída'néél'ąądígíí, saad da, haalzíidgo bikáá' ályaii, dóó baa hane'go aha'ádaalyaii

translations.definition: A collection of facts, such as numbers, words, measurements, observations or descriptions of thing

entries.sentence: When you write notes about something you see, like the number of petals on a flower, you are collecting data.

bee-hojileehii-akeedoo-heesdaii.definition: Hats'íís dóó ájít'éhígíí bił ho'dizhchínígíí hamá dóó hazhé'é bits'ą́ą́dę́ę́' nízhdiiláhígíí éí bik'ehgo náás oochíił'ii akéédóó naalnishígíí, recessive, bik'ehgo hojizhlį́į́'.

translations.definition: An inherited characteristic that appears in an offspring if it is received from a parent through a recessive allele. Compare with dominant trait.

entries.sentence: A recessive trait is determined by a recessive allele. For example, if you have an allele for light eyes and an allele for dark eyes you will have dark eyes because the allele for light eyes is recessive.

bee-iinaanii-alghaadziis.definition: Dahináanii t'ááłáhági haz'ą́ągo kééhat'į́įgo bee da'iináanii ałghadeidit'áahígíí

translations.definition: A negative interaction that occurs whenever two or more organisms require the same limited resource

entries.sentence: In the winter when there are few plants, the deer are in competition with one another for who gets to eat them.

bee-iinaanii-nidaalnish.definition: Hináanii bits'íístahdi t'áá ałtso ahił nidaalnishgo yee hiná dóó bits'íís bitł'óól hazhó'ó danéesé

translations.definition: In organisms, chemical reactions that help sustain life and allow cells to grow

entries.sentence: When you eat food, your body converts it into energy through a biochemical reaction called metabolism.

bee-iinei-iilini.definition: Hinááhts'ózí bitsą́ siléí ch'iyáán néidił'eeł dóó yee bidziil dóó biinéí yileehígíí

translations.definition: An organelle that takes in nutrients, breaks them down, and creates energy (ATP) for the cell

entries.sentence: Power plants supply electricity to your home, just like the mitochondria supplies energy to the cell.

bee-nanitinii.definition: Hináago naaldeeh dóó nidaat'a'ii binaagóó áhoot'éhígíí yee hadiit'įįhgo doo yit'į́į da yileeh

translations.definition: When organisms disguise their appearance to blend in with their surroundings

entries.sentence: Jackrabbits are brown so that they camouflage with the dirt and avoid being seen by predators.

bichiiya-taa-bi-iiliinii.definition: Hináanii t'áá bí ch'iyáán yidooyį́į́łígíí ííł' į́ t'áá ákǫ́ǫ́h binaagóó hólónígíí adinídíín dóó t'áádoole'é ałtah nihwiileehgo ahił nidaalnishígíí bits'ą́ą́ biziil choyooł'į́įgo

translations.definition: An organism capable of making its own food from inorganic substances using light or chemical energy (compare with heterotroph)

entries.sentence: You've never seen pine trees eat food because they are autotrophs and use light, water, and carbon dioxide to gain energy.

translations.definition: Any animal lacking a spinal cord (also known as a backbone). Compare with vertebrate.

entries.sentence: Worms, bees, and snails are all invertebrates.

biighaan-daholonigii.definition: Hináanii naaldeehii, animals, bits'íísts'in dóó bitsiighąą' bíígháán bii' danít'i' ígíí

translations.definition: An animal that has a spinal cord and a skeleton. Compare with invetebrate.

entries.sentence: Humans, horses, and birds are vertebrates, but an octopus has no bones or spinal cord and is thus an invertebrate.

bika-doo-biaadii-taa-geed-oochiil.word: biką' dóó bi'áadii t'áá gééd oochííł
bika-doo-biaadii-taa-geed-oochiil.definition: Azhé'é t'áágééd i'niitsąąh dóó ni'iichííh. Amá t'áá sáhá idínóoltsąął dóó adoołchííł.

translations.definition: The process of creating offspring without exchanging genetic information with another organism through sex.

entries.sentence: A bacteria cell can make a copy of itself by splitting into two cells.  When something reproduces without a partner, it is called asexual reproduction.

bikehgo-naalkaahii.definition: T'áá ha'át'íi da át'éegi dóó naalnishígi át'éego baa hane' dóó nábinázláago binahjį' áhá'nínígíí łą́ bééhózingo

translations.definition: An explanation for why things work or how things happen that is supported by a large amount of scientific evidence

entries.sentence: Cell theory states that all organisms are made up of cells and that all new cells are produced by existing cells. We know that this is true because we can see cells under a microscope and can trace the origin of new cells back to the cells they came from. There is no evidence to suggest this theory is incorrect.

translations.definition: A particular version of a gene.

entries.sentence: You have two copies of each gene in your genome.  If the copies are different, you have two alleles of that gene.

bikehgo-naas-oochiilii-lago-ate-siliiigi.word: bik'ehgo náás oochíiłii łago át'e silį́į́'ígíí t'áá át'é
bikehgo-naas-oochiilii-lago-ate-siliiigi.definition: Iiná bitł'óól bąąhgi bik'ehgo jinooséełgo t'áá jizį́į́ nít'ę́ę́' ájít'é dooleełígíí

translations.definition: A section of DNA that is the unit of heredity for a trait.

entries.sentence: In cats, fur length is determined by one gene, but body size is influenced by many genes.

translations.definition: A permanent alteration in the DNA sequence

entries.sentence: Mutations are common.  Every person has a lot of mutations, but most of them are not harmful.

bikehgo-naas-oochiilii-naaki-aheelte.definition: Bíla'ashdla'ii ła' biyik'ehgo náás oołchíiłii łahgo át'é silį́į́'ígíí naakigo bee hólǫ́.

translations.definition: An individual who has two of the same alleles for a gene. Compare with heterozygote.

entries.sentence: Individuals who have two sickle cell alleles are homozygotes at that locus and will display sickle cell disease.

bikehgo-naas-oochiilii-naaki-alaa-ate.word: bik'ehgo náás oochíiłii naaki ał'ąą át'é
bikehgo-naas-oochiilii-naaki-alaa-ate.definition: Bíla'ashdla'ii bi'iiná bitł'óól bąąhgi bik'ehgo náás oochíiłii gene naaki ał'ąą át'éego hólónígíí

translations.definition: An individual who has two different alleles of a gene. Compare with homozygote.

entries.sentence: Individuals who only have one sickle cell allele are heterozygotes at that locus and will not display sickle cell disease.

bikehgo-naas-oochiilii-naalkaah.definition: Dahináanii yik'ehgo náás oołchííłígíí dóó yił oołchíiłii nidaalkaahgo t'áá át'é bíhoo'aah

translations.definition: The study of genes and inheritance in living organisms

entries.sentence: A scientist who studies corn genetics researches how traits and diseases are inherited and affect yield.

bikehgo-naas-oochiiliii.definition: T'áá hoolzhishgi dahináanii t'áá sahdii dahyikahgo yik'ehgo náás oołchíiłii łahgo át'é daazlį́į'ígíí t'áá ałtso ááł jiní

translations.definition: All of the alleles of all the genes in a population of a specific species at a given time.

entries.sentence: A species with red and orange flowers has alleles for red and orange flower color in its gene pool.

translations.definition: A form of inheritance in which both alleles are expressed equally in the phenotype of the heterozygote

entries.sentence: When a chicken with white feathers breeds with a chicken with black feathers, the next generation has both black and white feathers because both alleles for feather color are codominant.

bil-hodizhchiinii-bee-jildziligii.definition: Bee ájít'éii bił ho'dizhchínígíí ła' bee hadziilgo jiinánígíí hái da nááná ła' ákót'éhígíí doo bił yizhchínígíí bił ałch'į' naa'nil aheełt'éego haz'ą́ągi haalzííd.

translations.definition: A heritable trait that increases the fitness of an individual with that trait compared to individuals without that trait in a particular environment.

entries.sentence: The Jackrabbit's large ears are an adaptation that helps them hear better and stay cool in the hot desert.

bil-naas-oochiil.definition: Dahináanii yizhchíigo bimá dóó bizhé'é yik'ehgo náás oołchíiłii bee bik'i' aschínígíí yee hadít'é yileeh

translations.definition: The passing on of traits from parents to offspring through genetic information.

entries.sentence: Scientific discoveries about heredity allow scientists to identify the causes of many genetic disorders.

bil-oochiilii-bee-hazhdiidzaa.definition: Hamá dóó hazhé'é bits'ą́ą́dę́ę́' bik'ehgo náás oochíiłii binahjį' yee hadít'éhígíí bił ho'dizhchį́įgo

translations.definition: To receive a particular characteristic from a parent, usually through genes that are passed from parent to offspring.

entries.sentence: When somone notices that siblings look alike, they are seeing similar traits that the siblings inherited from their parents.

bitsiniltlish-adin.definition: Atom yee hadít'éhígíí atsiniltł'ish bąąhgóó ádin.

translations.definition: The components of an atom that have no charge

entries.sentence: The nitrogen cycle describes the way nitrogen atoms move around the Earth.  Nitrogen atoms move around when organisms decompose, when bacteria absorb and release nitrogen, and when fertilizer used on crops washes into rivers and streams.

biyaazh-tsoasanigii.definition: Dahináanii bíígháán dahólónígíí  éí binaagóó áhoot'éhígíí binahjį' bits'íís sidoh dóó sik'azígíí éí (ectotherm wolyé). Yichííhgo táyi' naaleeh áádóó dahdínísééh dóó éí dóó  tábąąhdi dóó łeeshtahdi dahiná. Ch'ał ádaat'éii  dóó salamanders ááł ha'ní.

translations.definition: A vertebrate animal whose internal temperature depends on the environment (ectotherm). Most live in the water when young and live on land as adults. Examples include frogs, toads, and salamanders.

entries.sentence: Frogs are amphibians that live as tadpoles in the water when they are young, and are able to walk on land as an adult after they grow legs.

chidaiineeh.definition: Bíla'ashdla'ii dayikah nít'ę́ę́' yits'á dahanééhgo nááná łahjí dahyikahígíí yił yah adahnéehgo.

translations.definition: The migration of individuals out of a particular population or area to other populations or areas.  Compare with immigration.

entries.sentence: You could say that when you leave your classroom and walk out into the hall that you are emigrating from your classroom.

chil.definition: Nanise' ałtsaas'éí dóó ch'il shánídíín yee bidziilgo á'dólzingo hinánígíí.

translations.definition: An organism capable of photosynthesis

entries.sentence: Plants live in a variety of different environments. For example, pecan trees grow where it's warm, seaweed grow in the ocean, and piñon trees grow in or near the desert.

chil-doo-naaldloozhii-yiyani.definition: Hináanii ch'il dóó hináanii naaldeehígíí bich'iiyą' áyósinígíí.

translations.definition: An animal that eats both plants and animals

entries.sentence: People are ominvores because we can eat both meat and vegetables.

chil-dootlizhi-doo-dahinanii-sha-yee-bii.word: ch'il dootł'izhí dóó dahinánii shá yee biinéí
chil-dootlizhi-doo-dahinanii-sha-yee-bii.definition: Ch'il, nanise', dóó dahináanii ła' shánídíín yee bidziil dóó biinéígo áyósinígíí.

translations.definition: The process by which green plants and certain other organisms turns energy from the sun into chemical energy

entries.sentence: When plants photosynthesize, they absorb carbon dioxide and release oxygen that we can breathe.

chiyaan-bee-atsiis-haleehii.definition: Ch'iyáán ats'íís bee haleeh dóó hasht'éé yoodlííłígíí, protein, bee haleehígíí, amino acid, da'ahólzha' daaleeh dóó nidaat'ood łeh

translations.definition: A chain of amino acids that can fold into a specific shape

entries.sentence: Hemoglobin is the protein in a red blood cell that carries oxygen to different parts of your body.

chiyaan-bee-hahodite.definition: Ch'iyáán dahideezláádóó dahiníláajį' bee hahodít'éego nahaz'ą́. Łahgóó ahéédadiit'I'

translations.definition: Represents multiple pathways through which energy and nutrients flow through an ecosystem. It includes many intersecting food chains. It demonstrates that most organisms eat, and are eaten, by more than one species.

entries.sentence: Eagles eat mice, mice eat grain, and these three species form a food chain.

chiyaan-hinila.definition: Dahináanii ałkéé' bił honí'ą́ągo dahnaazhjaa'go bich'iiyą' nídanidlį́

translations.definition: A linear sequence of organisms through which nutrients and energy pass as one organism eats another

entries.sentence: A large, healthy deer that mates often will have higher fitness than a small, unhealthy deer that doesn't mate as often.

chiyaan-naalkaad-bee-jiinanigii.definition: Łikání hadił bitahdi hólǫ́ǫgo íiyisíí bee hadziil dóó hwiinéí áhósinígíí óolyé. Łikání glucose wolyéhígíí hadił bitahdi hólǫ́ éí hats'íís ííł'į́įgo hadił bitahdi niheleeh.
chiyaan-naalkaad-bee-jiinanigii.literal: chʼiyáán=food, bee jiinánígíí = keeping you alive... means "something about the food is keeping you alive"

translations.definition: The simple sugar that is the main source of energy. Glucose is found in the blood and is the main sugar that the body makes.

entries.sentence: Someone with diabetes needs to monitor their glucose levels, which is the amount of sugar that is in their bloodstream.

chiyaan-nayoolkaalii.definition: Atsą́ níléí ch'iyáán dóó jidlánígíí néedił'eel dóó bitoo' ádaat'éí yee ats'íís bidziil, biinéí, dóó hats'íís háidoodlííł. Ha'át'ííshį́į́ hats'íís doo choyooł'į́į da dooígíí éí hach'íí' bee níláhgóó kót'į́

translations.definition: The organs that take in food and liquids and break them down into substances that the body can use for energy, growth, and tissue repair. Waste products the body cannot use leave the body through bowel movements

entries.sentence: Your digestive system includes your mouth, stomach, and intestines. All of these organs play a role in breaking down food you can absorb nutrients and energy.

chiyaan-yidilidi.definition: Naaldeehii bits'íístahdi ch'iyáán yididiiłił go yee bidziil dóó hináadoogo éí ch'iyáán bits'íístahdi shoogishígíí yee yidiłidígíí hodooleeł éí ch'iyáán yidiiłtláadgo bits'ą́ą́dóó biinéí dóó bidziil.

translations.definition: A substance that acts as a catalyst in living organisms, regulating the rate at which chemical reactions proceed without itself being altered in the process

entries.sentence: People that are lactose intolerant cannot make the enzyme called lactase which prevents them from digesting lactose in milk.

chosh.definition: Hináanii ch'osh bidi'nínígíí éí hastą́ą́ bijáád dóó bits'a' dahólǫ́ǫgo yee bits'íís niłdzil éí exoskeleton wolyé.

translations.definition: Small animals with six legs and a hard outer shell called an exoskeleton.

entries.sentence: A ladybug is an insect because it has six legs and an outer shell; however, a tick is not an insect because it does not have six legs.

chosh-doo-yitiinii-bee-naatseedi.definition: Ch'osh doo yit'íinii bee yigání éí doodago bee ni'óolziní

translations.definition: A substance that kills or inhibits the growth of bacteria.

entries.sentence: When harmful bacteria make you sick, the doctor may give you antibiotics to get rid of the bacteria.

chosh-doo-yitinii.definition: Hináanii doo yit'íinii. Bee adéest'į́į́' dabidziilígíí t'éí bee daat'į́.

translations.definition: Microscopic single celled organisms.

entries.sentence: Even if your hands look clean it is important that you wash them to remove any harmful bacteria.

translations.definition: An organism too small to be seen without a microscope

entries.sentence: Sperm and egg cells are created through meiosis.

dahinaanii-bidziil.definition: Hináanii naayéé' doo bidééłníi da á'dólzin

translations.definition: The ability of an organism to withstand harmful effects

entries.sentence: Animals with thick fur have a resistance to cold temperatures.

dil.definition: Bíla'ashdla'ii bits'íístoo' dóó hináago naaldeehii bits'íístoo' bits'íístahgóó dóó bits'íís bitł'óól biyi'góó níłch'i dóó ch'iyáán dóó yee hináanii ayiił'eeł áádóó ats'íís ałtso choyoos'įįdii níláhgóó ats'á yiile

translations.definition: Body fluid in humans and other animals that delivers necessary substances such as nutrients and oxygen to the cells and transports waste away those cells

entries.sentence: Your body will stop functioning if it loses too much blood, because blood contains the oxygen and nutrients your body needs.

dil-alaa-adaatehigii.definition: Bíla'ashdla'ii bidił bee bééhózindooígíí ádaalyaa. Hadił łichíí'ígíí bikáá'gónaa áhoot'éhígíí bik'ehgo háidígííshį́į́ hadił A, B, O, doodago AB.

translations.definition: A system used to group human blood into different types, based on the presence or absence of certain characteristics on the surface of red blood cells. The four main blood types are A, B, O, and AB

entries.sentence: If you have surgery, it is important to know your blood type. If you lose a lot of blood, doctors find a match for your ABO blood type so that your body accepts the donated blood.

translations.definition: The non-cellular portion of blood

entries.sentence: Plasma makes up about half of our blood and has important salts, proteins, and antibodies that help fight infection.

translations.definition: The blood cells that carry oxygen

entries.sentence: Anemia occurs when someone doesn't have enough red blood cells.  It can cause people to feel tired, dizzy, or short of breath.

dil-ligaai.definition: Hinááhts'óóz ats'íís yee bihodééłníi da áyósin éí ii'noot'į́į́', infection, dóó ąąh dahwiidooł'aałii yich'ą́ą́h naalnish

translations.definition: Cells that are part of the body's immune system that help the body fight infection and other diseases.

entries.sentence: White blood cells are part of the immune system. There are many different types of white blood cells circulating in your blood and each type has a specific role in fighting infections.

dlaad.definition: Hináanii bich'iiyą' binaagóó hólónígíí néidił'eełgo bikágí biníkáágóne'  biihishigéeshgo yee hiná.

translations.definition: Living organisms that get their nutrients by absorbing them.

entries.sentence: The green or white patches on old bread are a type of fungi called mold.

doo-hinaanii.definition: Ha'át'íi da doo dahináa dago daholónígíí bee ályaa. Hináanii bits'ą́ą́dę́ę́'go éí organic át'ée doo.

translations.definition: Made of non-living materials. Compare with organic.

entries.sentence: Inorganic substances like metal and turquoise can be used to make a turquoise bracelet.

doo-lahgo-ayooliil-da.definition: Ha'át'íi da naalnishgo hoolzhish nít'ę́ę́' ni'áániił silį́į́' dóó bits'ą́ą́dóó doo łahgo áhooniił da yileeh éí náasdi hasht'e didooteeł

translations.definition: When the outcome of a process reduces further change and the process becomes stable

entries.sentence: The thermostat functions as a negative feedback loop because it keeps the temperature within a building stable by turning on the air conditioning if the temperature gets too hot or turning it off if it gets too cold.

doo-oochiil-ba-hazaa-da.definition: Haa'í da ha'át'íi da dahináanii t'ááłáhági bił haz'ą́ą doo nida'iiłchíigo hodeeshzhiizh

translations.definition: The situation where similar species may live in the same area, but they are unable to reproduce.

entries.sentence: Two populations of squirels on opposite sides of the Grand Canyon experienced reproductive isolation for so long that they are now two different species.

doo-yitiinii-bee-nelini.definition: Bee adéest'į́į́' ha'át'íi da áłts'íísígo doo yit'į́į da nidi nitsaago daat'į́įgo ádeile'

translations.definition: A device that makes very small objects or organisms larger so that they can be observed

entries.sentence: You need a microscope to see cells dividing.

translations.definition: The species that appear first in recently disturbed areas

entries.sentence: After a forest fire, the first species to reappear is a pioneer species.

haalzii-baa-haoodzii.definition: T'áádoole'é haalzíidgo haa da yit'ée doogo dóó bídí'nóol'ąął bohónéedzą́ baa ha'oodzíí'ígíí

translations.definition: A testable statement that explains a set of observations.

entries.sentence: You think bees prefer sunny days over cloudy days because it is unlikely to rain on sunny days.  You test your hypothesis by comparing the number of bees visiting flowers on both sunny and cloudy days.

haalziid.definition: Na'alkaahgo bééhózin silį́į'ii anáá' bee yitį́įgo haalzíídígíí.

translations.definition: The data measured or seen, often during an experiment

entries.sentence: You are making an observation when you feel a change in the weather, see a cloudy sky, or hear thunder.

haatii-da-doo-lahgo-aneehii.definition: Ha'át'íi da t'ááłáhági át'éego hadít'é dóó t'ááłáhági át'éego naalnishígíí

translations.definition: A substance that has a constant composition and characteristic properties.

entries.sentence: Did you know water is a chemical?  Each molecule of water contains two hydrogen atoms and one oxygen atom that bond together, which is why it is called H20.

haatii-da-dooniil-nawolni.definition: Haa da hoot'éedoo le' hasingo éí doodago na'alkaahgo naalkaahii ádoonííł nawólnínígíí

translations.definition: The chance that something will happen or of a hypothesis being correct

entries.sentence: When you role a six-sided die, the probability of rolling a three is 1/6, or 16.7%.

habil-doo-tsisee-hani-nili.definition: Niyiiłkaah dóó aná'át'áahjį' hats'íís biyi'di ajiłhosh, ninájílnish dóó hasht'ez dít'é bee bíhólníihgo nijigháhígíí

translations.definition: A natural, internal process that regulates daily cycles of sleep or inactivity and wakefulness or activity

entries.sentence: The circadian rhythm of sunflowers lets them anticipate the timing and direction of the sunrise each morning so that they can face their flowers towards the sun.

hadaate.definition: Nahasdzáán biyi'dę́ę́' t'áádoole'é doo dahináanii bits'ą́ą́dę́ę́' hólónígíí

translations.definition: An inorganic substance that occurs naturally

entries.sentence: Precious stones like onyx and turquiose are minerals.

hai-da-bi-allele-taa-altso.word: hái da bi allele t'áá ałtso
hai-da-bi-allele-taa-altso.definition: Ha'át'íi da hináanii yik'ehgo náás oołchíiłii łahgo át'é daazlį́į́'ígíí t'áá ałtso

translations.definition: All of the alleles in a given individual's genome

entries.sentence: A sheep's genotype may include alleles for white wool and black wool, but because the black allele is dominant, the sheep's phenotype will be black wool.

haigo-iilhoshii.definition: Haigo éí doodago ch'iyáán doo łą́ dahólǫ́ǫ da haleehgo na'at'i'í dóó naaldlooshii bits'íís ni'át'įįh, doo ayóo naha'ná da, doo ayóo sizil da,

translations.definition: A state in which an animal’s body processes are slower than usual, its body temperature falls, and its activities are limited, usually during winter or when food is scarce.

entries.sentence: Many animals that live in the mountains hibernate because there is little food in the cold winter months.

haniihii.definition: Dahináanii t'ááła' dahyikahgo ts'ídá bá yá'áhoot'éehgo haz'ą́ągi yee hináanii bá hólǫ́ǫgo t'éí náás yikah

translations.definition: A species that requires specific environmental conditions or specific types of resources. Compare with generalist.

entries.sentence: Yucca plants are specialists that are only pollinated by specific species of moths. These moths are also specialists because they can only lay their eggs in yucca flowers.

hatsoos.definition: Hats'íístahgóó hadił nidaazlį́įgo bee níłch'i dóó ch'iyáán hats'íís bitah hólǫ́ǫgo áyósin áádóó hats'íís ałtso choyoos'įįdígíí hats'áyiiléego jiinánígíí. Kót'éego binaanishígíí éí hajéídíshjool, hats'oos łichí'í dóó hats'oos dootł'izhí.

translations.definition: The system that moves blood, oxygen, nutrients, waste, and other materials throughout the body. The circulatory system includes the heart, arteries, and veins

entries.sentence: When you have high blood pressure, your circulatory system doesn't function as well becuase your heart has to work harder to pump the same amount of blood through your body.

hinaahtsooz.definition: Ats'íís bitł'óól éí ats'íís bee ályaaígíí ts'ídá t'ááłá'ígo si'ánígíí náhidínísé, binaagóó yaa ákonízin, dóó ádéé'ál'į́įgo noosééł.
hinaahtsooz.literal: hinááh = life, ts'óózí=small. Means "small unit of life"

translations.definition: The cell is the smallest unit with the basic properties of life, such as growth, sensing the environment, and reproduction.

entries.sentence: Your body is composed of around 37 trillion cells! That is a big number but there are even more bacteria cells that live on and in you: around 100 trillion.

hinaahtsooz-ahii-sila.definition: Hinááhts'ózí t'áá ahedaałt'éego dóó binaanish t'ááłá'ígo

translations.definition: A group of cells that perform a similar function

entries.sentence: Gum disease causes your gum tissue to become red, swollen, and bleed easily.  You can prevent gum disease by frequently brushing and flossing your teeth.

hinaahtsooz-altsa-nooseel.definition: Hinááhts'óóz ał'ąą át'éego ałts'á níséego, mitosis éí doodago meiosis, alą́ąjį' bee hahalzhííshígíí

translations.definition: The first stage of mitosis or meiosis where chromosomes are compressed

hinaahtsooz-naaghaii.definition: Hináanii hinááhts'óóz t'ááłá'ígo bits'íís nilį́. Hináanii náádaała' éí hinááhts'óóz łą́ągo yee bits'íís dahólǫ́.

translations.definition: Organisms that consist of one cell. Compare with multicellular.

entries.sentence: Bacteria are unicellular organisms because they are made of one cell.

hinaahtsozi.definition: Ats'íís bitł'óól éí ats'íís bee ályaaígíí ts'ídá t'ááłá'ígo si'ánígíí náhidínísé, binaagóó yaa ákonízin, dóó ádéé'ál'į́įgo noosééł.
hinaahtsozi.literal: hinááh = life, ts'óózí=small. Means "small unit of life"

translations.definition: The cell is the smallest unit with the basic properties of life, such as growth, sensing the environment, and reproduction.

entries.sentence: Your body is composed of around 37 trillion cells! That is a big number but there are even more bacteria cells that live on and in you: around 100 trillion.

hinaahtsozi-adeeiiyaa.definition: Hinááhts'ózí t'ááłá'í si'ánígíí ałts'á níséehgo naaki yileehígíí

translations.definition: When a single cell divides into two identical cells

entries.sentence: As we grow, our cells divide through mitosis to create new cells.

hinaahtsozi-altsa-niseego-iina-bitlool-a.word: hinááhts'ózí ałts'á níséego iiná bitł'óól ałkéé' niikah
hinaahtsozi-altsa-niseego-iina-bitlool-a.definition: Hinááhts'ózí t'ááłáhádi éí doodago naakidi ałts'áníséego iiná bitł'óól ałníi'gi hasht'e' niikáahgo hodinaahígíí.

translations.definition: The time during mitosis or meiosis where copies of chromosomes line up in the center of a cell

hinaahtsozi-bijei.definition: Hinááhts'óóz yee naalnishgo biyi'di iiná bitł'óól ak'ah bineestiin, plasma. bik'í da'asti'go daholǫ́.

translations.definition: The part of the cell that controls the functions of the cell and where DNA is found within a plasma membrane

entries.sentence: Most cells of organisms that are eukaryotes have a nucleus in their cells.  In animals, red blood cells do not have a nucleus.

translations.definition: Between or among cells

entries.sentence: If the rooms of your house are cells, the hallways between the rooms are like intercellular space.

hinaahtsozi-bitsasilei.definition: Hinááhts'óóz/ats'íís bitł'óól biyi'di si'ą́ągo bik'ésti'ii hólǫ́ áádóó t'áá ha'át'ííshį́į́ binaanish.

translations.definition: A structure within a cell that is enclosed within its own membrane and has a function

entries.sentence: Organelles in a cell are like organs in a body.  Cell organelles include the nucleus and mitochondria.

hinaahtsozi-biyi-di-bijei-binaagoo-holon.word: hinááhts'ózí biyi' di bijéí binaagóó hólónígíí
hinaahtsozi-biyi-di-bijei-binaagoo-holon.definition: Ats'íís bitł'óól biyi' hólónígíí t'áá át'é ákondi bijéí nucleus éí doo bił ólta'góó

translations.definition: All of the contents of a cell excluding the nucleus

entries.sentence: If we think of a cell as a jello salad, the cytoplasm is like the jello because it suspends and holds the contents inside.

hinaahtsozi-iina-bitlool-naaki-bee-holo.word: hinááhts'ozí iiná bitł'óól naaki bee hólǫ́
hinaahtsozi-iina-bitlool-naaki-bee-holo.definition: Ats'íís bitł'óól éí doodago hináanii bi iiná bitł'óól ałk'í daadeesdizígíí chromosomes naaki níláago bee dahólónígíí

translations.definition: A cell or organism having two sets of chromosome

entries.sentence: Most cells in our body are diploid and have a copy of each chromosome from both of our parents.

hinaahtsozi-nooseel.definition: Hinááhts'óóz dínéesą́ą́dóó biyi'di iiná bitł'óól bééda'alne' silį́į'jį

translations.definition: The period of time when a cell grows and copies of DNA are made.

entries.sentence: Cells spend most of their time in interphase, before making preparations to divide.

hinaan-taa-la-yikahigii.definition: Hináanii t'áá ałhąąh daazhchí dóó danihinánígíí, Amá dóó ach'é'é éí naakidi azhchínígíí át'é

translations.definition: All of the organisms born and living at about the same time. In humans, mother and daughters represent two generations

entries.sentence: During elementary school, all the kids in your class are a part of your generation because all of you have similar ages.

translations.definition: A living thing

entries.sentence: Animals, fungi, bacteria, and plants are all examples of organisms.

translations.definition: How an organism acts

entries.sentence: A spider building a web, a dog wagging its tail, or a bird calling for a mate are all examples of these organism's behavior.

hinaanii-achi-nahwiilna.definition: Haa'í da t'áá íídą́ą́' yá'át'éehgo haz'ą́ągo hináanii nááná łahdę́ę́' yikahgo yah ee'nil nít'ę́ę́' ákwe'é dahinánígíí dóó haz'ánígíí yich'į' nidahwiilná silį́įgo

translations.definition: A species that was introduced to an ecosystem that was not there before and that causes hardship to other organisms Haa

entries.sentence: Russian olive trees and buffelgrass are invasive species that outcompete native species and negatively affect of the environment.

hinaanii-ahil-nidaalnish.definition: Nahasdzáán bikáá'góó hináanii kéédahat'į́įgo bił nahaz'ánígíí hináanii t'áá át'é yiłdi'ígíí

translations.definition: Any living thing that has an effect on another organism, population, community, or ecosystem

entries.sentence: Plant growth can be affected by biotic factors like herbivores and pathogens.

hinaanii-alaa-adaatehigii.definition: Iiná ał'ąą ádaat'éego bits'ą́ą́dóó ayoonaałígíí éí díí dahináanii t'áá ałtso, bik'ehgo yee oołchíiłii dóó naaldeehgo bá nahaz'áą́góó dóó hózhǫ́ǫgo bá dahoo'a'gi

translations.definition: The variety of life and its processes, including the variety of living organisms, the genetic differences among them, and the communities and ecosystems in which they occur

entries.sentence: The biodiversity in a desert includes the cacti, bats, lizards, hummingbirds, and beetles that live there.

hinaanii-alaa-ate-ahilka-anaahinilcheeh.definition: Hináanii doo t'ááła' nilį́į da nidi ahíłká aná'ahinilchxéehgo

translations.definition: Association between organisms of two different species in which each benefits

entries.sentence: A mutualism occurs between people and the bacteria that live in our intestines.  The bacteria gain nutrients while they help us digest food.

hinaanii-altso-iildeeh.definition: Dahináanii ła' ha'át'ííshį́į́ biniinaa áádįįł silį́į'go

translations.definition: The act or process of a species becoming extinct

entries.sentence: There are very few black footed ferrets still alive and they are at risk for extinction.

translations.definition: A characteristic of an organism that can be observed.

entries.sentence: Hair color, eye color, and nose size are all traits you inherit from your parents.

hinaanii-bee-hahoditeegi.definition: Nahasdzáán bikáá'góó áhoołts'óózígo honít'i'go iiná bináhaazlá

translations.definition: The thin zone surrounding Earth where all life exists

entries.sentence: The biosphere includes all of the places on Earth where organisms can live.

hinaanii-beiina-bitlool-taa-altso.definition: Ha'át'íi da hináanii bits'íís bitł'óól t'áá ałtso bits'íís bii' hólónígíí

translations.definition: All of an organism's DNA

entries.sentence: We can compare the genomes of different people to see if we can identify which alleles cause diseases.

hinaanii-bihinaahtsozi-bijei-daholonigii.definition: Ha'át'íi da hinááts'óóz t'ááła'í si'ą́ągo bits'íís nilį́įgo biyi'di bijéí, nucleus doo hólǫ́ǫ da

translations.definition: Organisms whose cells contain a nucleus. Compare with prokaryote.

entries.sentence: Most of the living things you see around you, including all plants and animals, are eukaryotes.

translations.definition: A unicellular organism that does not have a nucleus and has few organelles.  Compare with eukaryote.

entries.sentence: The bacteria in your gut are prokaryotes that help you digest food and defend against pathogens.

hinaanii-bil-haza.definition: Dahináanii kééhat'į́įgi áádóó ákwe'é haz'ą́ągi t'áá ałtsoní bee hahodít'éego haz'ą́

translations.definition: An environment including every living organism and non-living factor that is contained within it

entries.sentence: Deserts, rain forests, and and coral reefs are all ecosystems with distinct climates and species.

translations.definition: Place where an organism or a community of organisms lives, including all living and nonliving factors or conditions of the environment

entries.sentence: Some animal species can only survive in a specific habitat, like roadrunners that live in the deserts, brushlands, and grasslands of North America.

hinaanii-bitsa.definition: Hináanii bits'a' dahólónígíí bits'íís bits'a' yee naazį́ dóó naaghá.

translations.definition: A hard covering on the outside of the body used for support, protection, and muscle attachment. Examples include the shells of snails and the outer covering of beetles.

entries.sentence: Scorpions have an exoskeleton that protects them from predators.

translations.definition: Made of or made by living organisms. Compare with inorganic.

entries.sentence: Organic substances like wood and animal hides can be used to make a drum.

translations.definition: Evidence of living organisms that lived long ago

entries.sentence: Dinosaur bones are fossils and they tell us when and where dinosaurs used to live.

hinaanii-chil-doo-naaldlooshii-yiyanigii.definition: Ha'át'íi da hináanii bidziil dóó yee hináadoo biniyé ch'il/nanise' dóó níláhgóó dahináanii yiyą́

translations.definition: An organism that eats plants or animals for energy and nutrients (compare with autotroph)

entries.sentence: People, insects, fish, and birds are examples of heterotrophs because they must eat plants and animals to survive.

hinaanii-chiyaan-nili.definition: Hináanii ayą́ągo hiná dóó áadi ha'át'ííshį́į́ bich'iiyą' nánídlį́į́góne'

translations.definition: An organism's position along a food chain

entries.sentence: Plants are at the base of each food chain.  Animals that eat plants, like deer, are one trophic level above plants.  Animals that eat deer, like mountain lions, are one trophic level above deer.

hinaanii-daaheelchiih.definition: Dahináanii t'ááłá'í danilį́įgo ałhá nida'iiłchíigo noosééł

translations.definition: A group of organisms that can reproduce with one another and produce fertile offspring

entries.sentence: All dogs are the same species, but a dog and a cat are different species because they cannot mate and produce offspring together.

hinaanii-dildzid.definition: Hináanii ła' daaztsą́ągo ch'osh doo yit'íinii dóó dláád ádaat'éii yaa tįįh yikaigo ałtso dííłdzidgo

translations.definition: The breakdown of dead organism through the action of bacteria and fungi

entries.sentence: The mold we see on old fruit is a type of fungus that decays the fruit.

hinaanii-dildzidgo-leezh-naadleel.definition: Ha'át'íi da hináá nít'ę́ę'go ádin silį́į́' dóó bits'íís táánoodééh dóó dííyahgo ła' łeezh nídaasdlį́į́' dóó bits'iilzéí dahólǫ́.

translations.definition: The process by which dead organic substances are broken down into simpler organic or inorganic material

entries.sentence: The leaves that fall on the ground during autum are important because they decompose and become a part of the soil, which helps other plants grow.

hinaanii-doo-yitiinii-baate-holoonii.definition: Naałniih éí doodago ch'osh doo yit'íinii ba'át'e' hólǫ́ǫgo ats'íís biyi'di yihodiiłt'ihii.

translations.definition: A microorganism that causes, or can cause, disease or harm in the organism it lives in or on (its host)

entries.sentence: The pathogen COVID-19 affects your lungs which can give you a fever and make it hard to breathe.

hinaanii-eelini.definition: Ha'át'íi da hináanii nááná ła' hináanii yenoolnin, biinéé' yił aheełt'é, éí doodago yeel'į́įgo naaghá.

translations.definition: When one organism looks, sounds, or behaves like another species.

entries.sentence: Stick insects use mimicry to trick predators into thinking they are twigs.

hinaanii-hinaahtsooz-laago-yee-hadite.definition: Hináanii bits'íís hinááhts'ózí łą́ągo yee hadít'éhígíí. Ła'éí hinááhts'ózí t'ááłá'ígo bits'íís nilį́.

translations.definition: Organisms that consist of more than one cell. Compare with unicellular.

entries.sentence: Horses, mushrooms, and cacti are multicellular organisms because they are made of many cells.

hinaanii-keyah-choyooli.definition: Naaldlooshii haashį́į́ néelt'e'go íiyisíí kéyah bikáá'go da' ayą́ągo bihonéedzą́

translations.definition: Maximum population size of the species that the environment can sustain, given the food, habitat, water, and other necessities available in the environment

entries.sentence: If you use too many apps on your cell phone at once, it will slow down or stop working.  Similarly, the environment has a carrying capacity and can only support a certain number of organisms, otherwise the population size will decrease.

hinaanii-la-bil-ahooya.definition: Ha'át'íi da ałtso eeldee' éí doodago bił ahóóyáago doo ła' yidziih da silį́į́'ígíí

translations.definition: Occurs when there are no more individuals of that species alive anywhere in the world

entries.sentence: Wooly mammoths died thousands of years ago and this species is now extinct.

hinaanii-oochiil-yaa-ninisa.definition: Bíla'ashdla'ii oochííł yaa ninísą́ągo yaa hóyánígíí.

translations.definition: An organism that has reached sexual maturity.

entries.sentence: A cat is an adult once it can have kittens.

hinaanii-taa-altso-yinildzilii.definition: Hoolzhishgo ał'ąą ánáhoo'níiłgo hináanii yídaníłdzilígíí náás yikah dóó oołchíiłgo bits'íís hadidzaaígíí ayóo yit'į́. Łahgo át'éego éí hináanii bił áhoot'éhígíí doo yíníłdzilgóó éí t'óó áádįįł dóó bits'íís doo łahgo áániił da doo.

translations.definition: A species that can thrive in many different environmental conditions or use different types of resources. Compare with specialist.

entries.sentence: A raccoon is an example of a generalist.  It lives in a variety of habitats, from cities to forests, and it eats a lot of different types of food.

translations.definition: The process where organisms with the highest fitness in an environment survive and reproduce and their phenotypes become more common in the next generation. At the same time, organisms with the lowest fitness fail to survive or reproduce less and their phenotypes become less common in the next generation.

entries.sentence: Natural selection will favor desert mice with light fur because they are less likely to be seen by predators.

hinanii-chiyaan-ba-silaii.definition: Dahináanii shánídíín choyooł'į́įgo yee biinéí dóó bidziil dahólǫ́ éí ts'ídá binahjį' ch'iyáán nihwiileeh

translations.definition: Organisms that are capable of photosynthesis and are the basis of a food chain.

entries.sentence: A common food chain in a field includes grass, which are producers that can be eaten by grasshoppers, which then can be eaten by other animals.

hohoneedza.definition: Hináanii biyázhí yishchínígíí yisdádoogáałgo bidziilgo hináadoo

translations.definition: The ability of an individual to produce successful offspring compared to other individuals of the same species.

entries.sentence: In many species, organisms pass on their DNA to their offspring when an egg and sperm cell combine during fertilization.

translations.definition: Barren area of land where little precipitation occurs

entries.sentence: The Sonoran Desert is found in the southwestern United States and the species that live in it can survive long periods of time with little to no rain.

iigash.definition: Hináanii biką'ígíí bik'eelyéí. Dabi'áádígíí ałdó' bik'eelyéí hólǫ́, biyeezhii (egg) deiłní.

translations.definition: The male sex cell, or gamete in animals.  Compare with egg.

entries.sentence: Car accidents are the leading cause of injuries to the spinal cord. If damaged, the spinal cord cannot repair itself.  This is why it is important to wear your seatbelt.

iina-ba-yaahooteehgo-haza.definition: Nahasdzáán bikáagi haashį́į́ honíłtso haz'ą́ągo nanise' dóó naaldeehii bá yá'áhoot'éehgo bił hoolzhish

translations.definition: A regional or global land area that is characterized by the plants, animals and climate in that area

entries.sentence: A desert is a biome that has plants and animals that live successfully with little or no rainfall.

iina-bitlool.definition: Ats'íís bitł'óól nineezgo hats'íís biyi'di hólónígíí bik'ehgo náás oochíiłii yee hadít'é. Bik'ehgo ch'iyáán ats'íís ánáyoodlíiłii proteins hats'íístahgóó hólónígíí nihwiileeh.

translations.definition: A long molecule that contains our unique genetic information. It holds the instructions for making all of the proteins in our bodies.

entries.sentence: Construction workers need instructions to know how to build a building.  Cells use DNA as instructions to tell them what to do and how to make things.

iina-bitlool-adeealneeh.definition: Ats'íís bitł'óól ałts'á hiniséego iiná bitł'óól biyi'di dahólónígíí bééda'alne'

translations.definition: The process by which DNA is copied during cell division

entries.sentence: In humans, DNA is about 6 billion base pairs long. DNA needs to be copied accurately each time a cell divides. Sometimes there can be an error in the DNA replication process. This is one way mutations can arise.

iina-bitlool-aheelte.definition: Iiná bitł'óól, DNA dóó RNA t'áá áłah ałkéé' danéedééhígíí, ats'íís bee haleehii proteins, éí doodago bik'ehgo ázhnoolnin dóó ájít'é yileehígíí ahedaałt'éego

translations.definition: Similarity of DNA or RNA sequences, proteins, or traits due to shared ancestry

entries.sentence: Bees, ants, and wasps share a homologous trait of three-part bodies (abdomen, thorax, and head) because they have shared ancestry.

iina-bitlool-bee-alyaii.definition: Iiná bitł'óól, DNA dóó RNA bee haleehígíí (base deiłníigo hináanii bee hodooleełígíí)

translations.definition: The building blocks of the RNA and DNA, also known as 'bases'. The symbols for the four bases are A, T, G, and C.

entries.sentence: The nucleus of an oxygen atom contains 8 protons and 8 neutrons.

iina-bitlool-bitoh.definition: Iiná bitł'óól nídahineeshzhee'go ats'íís bitł'óól biyi'di dahólónígíí

translations.definition: A structure made up of DNA tightly coiled many times around proteins

entries.sentence: Humans have two copies of 23 chromosomes.  People who have an extra copy of one or part of their 21st chromosome are born with a condition called Down Syndrome.

iina-bitlool-lahgo-adzaii-binaalniih.definition: Bik'ehgo náás oochíiłii ałkéé' sinilyę́ę łahgo ádzaago ba'át'e' hazlį́į'go bits'ą́ą́dóó ąąh dahaz'ą́ bił oochííł yileeh.

translations.definition: A disease that is caused by a change (mutation) in an individual's DNA sequence

entries.sentence: A disease like xeroderma pigmentosum makes you sensitive to sunlight and is a genetic disorder because it can be inherited from your parents.

iina-bitlool-lahgo-alyaa.definition: Ha'át'íi da hináanii bits'íís dóó bíni' da yee hadít'éhígíí t'áá ákólnéehgo be'iiná bitł'óól łahgo ál'įįhígíí

translations.definition: The deliberate modification of the characteristics of an organism by changing its DNA or RNA

entries.sentence: There are types of corn that have had their DNA genetically engineered so that they can resist insect herbivores.  Organisms that have had their DNA engineered are called genetically modified organisms (GMOs).

iina-bitlool-taa-lai.definition: Hinááts'ózí éí doodago hináanii bits'íístahdi iiná bitł'óól ałk'ídeesdizígíí chromosomes t'ááłáhájí t'éí biyi'di hólǫ́

translations.definition: A cell or organism having a single set of chromosomes

entries.sentence: Unlike most cells in our body, egg and sperm cells are haploid and have one copy of each chromosome.

iina-dooleelii.definition: Bik'ehgo náás oochíiłii iiná bitł'óól ałk'ídeesdizígíí, chromosome, bee at'ééd éí doodago ashkii jileehígíí bąąhgi hólónígíí

translations.definition: A gene that is located on a sex chromosome

entries.sentence: Humans have two sex chromosomes called X and Y.  The genes that cause red-green color blindness are sex-linked genes because are passed down on the X chromosome.  Males inherit only one X chromosome, and all alleles on that chromosome act as dominant alleles.  This is why red-green color blindness is more common in men.

iina-naalkaah.definition: Dahináanii t'áá ałtso baa nahodigį'go naalkaah
iina-naalkaah.literal: iiná=life, naalkaah= study. means to study life.

translations.definition: The scientific study of life and living organisms

entries.sentence: In biology class, you learn how all of life functions and changes over time, from the smallest celled organisms to vast ecosystems.

iniiyaa.definition: T'áádoole'é bááhádzidii tł'óó'di nahaz'ą́ą́góó yahiigeehgo. Díí bááhádzidii pollutants wolyé.

translations.definition: The introduction of harmful materials into the environment. These harmful materials are called pollutants.

entries.sentence: Car exhaust is a type of pollution that can cause lung damage when animals breathe it in.

keedilyeeh.definition: Dá'ák'eh, k'eelyéí, daneest'áanii, dóó iiná naalyé bíhoo'ą́ą́'
keedilyeeh.comment: this applies to all types of vegetation. Word will change If start talking about specific planting of something ie "plant corn, kil dilyeeh"

translations.definition: The art, science, or practice of cultivating the soil, growing crops, and raising livestock

entries.sentence: Successful agriculture depends on farmers knowing the right time of year to plant their seeds, the correct soil, and how much water their plants need to grow.

translations.definition: The female sex cell, or gamete. Compare with sperm.

keyah-biiiineeh-doo-haaiineeh.definition: Bíla'ashdla'ii kéédahat'į́įgi yah ahekááh dóó ch'éédahakááh.

translations.definition: The movement of individuals in and out of a population or area

entries.sentence: To find food, geese migrate to the north during the summer and migrate back to the south during the winter.

keyah-bikaagi-hazanigi.definition: Dahináanii dóó naaldeehii bá haz'ą́ągi níłch'I, tó, dóó kéyah yee dahinánígíí

translations.definition: The air, water, and land in or on which all living organisms live

entries.sentence: Cacti can survive in hot and dry environments because they are good at storing and conserving water.

lahgo-haza-bii-anooneel.definition: Haa'í da diné kééhat'į́įgo daashį́į́ néelt'e' ákwe'é kééhát'į́ éí daashį́į́ néelt'e' haashį́į́ nízahjį' nihoolzhiizhgo daneeznánígíí/bikéé' ádahasdįįd yéélta'ígíí ałch'į' sinilgo baa hane'

translations.definition: The number of total deaths in comparison to the total population size in a specified community or area over a specified period of time

entries.sentence: If 20 cows die on a farm with 100 total cows, then we could say that 0.2 cows die for every cow on the farm (20 calves / 100 cows = 0.2 calves per cow).

likani-bichi-iinei.definition: Ch'iyáán íiyisíí bee ats'íís bidziil dóó biinéí áyósinígíí. Łikání t'éí át'é.

translations.definition: An important nutrient used as energy by the body and made mostly of sugars

entries.sentence: Your body can use the carbohydrates  in foods like bread and potatoes  as sources of energy.

translations.definition: The main form of stored carbohydrates in plants.

entries.sentence: Traditional buffalo hunting was once a sustainable hunting method because hunting buffalo did not negatively affect their population.
