lizh-ba-hazaagi.definition: Ats'íís biyi'di siláhígíí ats'íís ałtso choyoos'įįdígíí níláhgóó yits'ą́ąjį' kwííł'ínígíí

translations.definition: The organs that remove waste from the body.

entries.sentence: The urinary system is the filter of the bloodstream as it captures waste and releases it from the body.

naaldeehii.definition: Hináanii bits'íís bitł'óól (cells) daholónígíí biyi'di bijéí (nucleus) iiná bitł'óól dóó bik'ehgo náás oochíiłii nídahineeshzhee'.

translations.definition: A large group of multicellular eukaryotic organisms.

entries.sentence: People, butterflies, fish, and cats are all examples of animals.

naaldeehii-bil-haza.definition: Hináanii ał'ąą ádaat'éhígíí bił haz'ą́ągi ahił dahi'ná dóó binaagóó áhoot'éhígíí chodayooł'ínígíí

translations.definition: The study of how organisms interact with one another and with their environment

entries.sentence: Emissions from coal plants can affect the ecology of an ecosystem by polluting the environment and affecting how organisms survive and interact with each other.

translations.definition: Animals that eat other animals

entries.sentence: Mountain lions have very sharp teeth because they are carnivores and need to tear apart the animals they eat.

naaldeehii-naalkaah.definition: Hináanii naaldeehii, animals, ał'ąą át'éego dahyikahígíí haalzííd dóó naalkaahgo bíhoo'aah

translations.definition: The scientific study of animals

entries.sentence: Ranchers use their zoology knowledge to breed sheep so their offspring have more meat or fluffier wool.

naaldeehii-yee-sizili.definition: Hináanii naaldeehii binaagóó áhoot'éhígíí bik'ehgo bits'íístahdi sizil dóó náhiniik' áás

translations.definition: An organism whose internal temperature depends on the temperature of their surrounding environment

entries.sentence: Ectotherms, like lizards, can't control their body temperature and often lay in the sun to warm up.

naaldlooshii.definition: Hináanii bits'íís bitł'óól (cells) daholónígíí biyi'di bijéí (nucleus) iiná bitł'óól dóó bik'ehgo náás oochíiłii nídahineeshzhee'.
naaldlooshii.literal: living things that trot or walk

translations.definition: A large group of multicellular eukaryotic organisms.

entries.sentence: People, butterflies, fish, and cats are all examples of animals.

naaldlooshii-chil-yilchozhigii.definition: Na'at'i'í éí doodago naaldlooshii chil deiłchozhígíí

translations.definition: Animals that eat plants

entries.sentence: Rabbits are herbivores that only eat leaves, seeds, twigs, and other parts of plants.

naalkaah.definition: Na'alkaah bee éé'deetánígíí náás hideesnilgo íhoo'aah bínááhiniidééh

translations.definition: The creation of new knowledge or the use of existing knowledge in a new way so as to create new concepts, methods, and understandings

entries.sentence: Decades of scientific research on vaccine ingredients and side effects has shown that vaccines do not cause autism.

naalkaah-bibeehazaanii.definition: Ałkéé' honí'ą́ągo na'alkaah bee doo naaki nilį́į́góó éé'deetį́į́h dóó íhoo'aah. I'diił'áii bééhózingo ál'įįh, át'éedoo dóó nídleeł baa nitáháskéezgo saad ál'įįh, díí baa nitsáháskéézígíí naalkaah,

translations.definition: A way for scientists to study and learn things. In the first step, a problem is identified, then a hypothesis is made from existing information, and then the hypothesis is tested with an experiment.

entries.sentence: Scientists have hypothesized that plants can absorb metal contamination. Following the scientific method, they conducted an experiment to test their hypothesis and observed that metal contaminants can be found in plant tissues.

naalkaahgone.definition: Haa'í da haz'ą́ągo ákone' éé'deetįįh biniyé nida'alkaah, nida'nitin, éí doodago azee' dóó t'áá ha'át'ííshį́į́ béésh da, tó da, níłch'i da ałtah ádaalne'go bee t'áadoole'é ádaalne'

translations.definition: A place for science experiments, teaching, or the making of medicine or chemicals

entries.sentence: Any space set aside for performing science experiments is a laboratory. This could even include your own backyard.

naalkaahi.definition: Na'alkaah yeda'aniihgo éé'deetįįh yinidaalnish dóó t'áá ha'át'ííshį́į́ hiná éí doodago naalnishgi át'áo yéé'dítį́į́h. Hada'asííd, ída'neeł'ąąh, dóó bił bééhoozinígíí yaa dahalne'.
naalkaahi.literal: Naʼałkaahíí=thing that does the investigating "Reseacher" . naʼałkaah=research, the íí is what makes it describing.

translations.definition: People who discover new things and research how things work. They observe, measure, and communicate results

entries.sentence: Not all scientists work in a lab.  Anyone who uses observations and research to answer a question is a scientist. That can even include you!

naalniih.definition: Hináanii doo yit'į́į dago nááná łahdę́ę́' yigáałgo ats'íís bihinááhts'óóz biyi'di hiná silį́į́', dóó áadi hinááhts'óóz yee binahjį' noosééł yileeh áádóó bits'ą́ą́dóó ąąh dahaz'ą́ haleeh

translations.definition: A small parasite that lives within the cells of other organisms, cannot reproduce without the help of the cell it lives inside of, and causes disease.

entries.sentence: Hantavirus is a virus that causes serious lung disease. You can catch hantavirus from the saliva, urine, or droppings of deer mice and other rodents.

naalniih-ihodiilti.definition: Ba'át'e' hólóonii (pathogen) bits'ą́ą́dóó ii'noot'į́į́' doodago łóód haleehgo doo ákójít'ée da jileehígíí: naałniih (virus), ch'osh doo yit'íinii, éí doodago yaa' ádaat'éii.

translations.definition: An illness caused by a pathogen, such as a virus, bacteria, or parasite.

entries.sentence: Our immune system is made up of white blood cells and tissues like our skin which can protect us from bacterial and viral infections

naalzheehii.definition: Dahináanii hináanii náánáła' neiłtseed dóó yiyą́ąyee hiná

translations.definition: An organism that lives mostly by killing and eating other animals

entries.sentence: Mountain lions are predators because they must hunt other animals to survive.

naalzheehii-naaltseed.definition: Hináanii dahináanii nááná ła' neitseed dóó yiyą́ągo.

translations.definition: The killing and eating of one organism (the prey) by another (the predator)

entries.sentence: Predation occurs when a lizard eats a fly, a cat eats a mouse, or a bear eats a fish.

naas-ahoolchiilii-yildil.definition: Náás oochííł, bits'ą́ą́dę́ę́' oodáał ii naaltsoos bikáá' naashch'ąą'

translations.definition: A drawing that shows how ancestors and descendents are related

entries.sentence: You can track the pedigree of a horse if you know who the parents and grandparents were.  This information lets you keep your horses from interbreeding.

nahasdzaan-bikaagoo-honiidooh.definition: Nahasdzáán bikáá'góó sidooígíí de'áániił. Níłch'i hado yił hidideełígíí, greenhouse gases, łą́ dahshoogish silį́į́'ígíí biniinaa nahasdzáán ayóo sido silį́į́'.

translations.definition: The increase in the Earth's average temperature. Recently, temperatures are rising rapidly due to increases in the levels of greenhouse gases.

entries.sentence: One of the results of burning fossil fuels is global warming, which is causing a rise in the average temperature of the planet.

nahasdzaan-iina-anayoodliil.definition: Níłch'i nitrogen, t'eesh carbon dóó t'áádoole'é doo dahináanii bits'ą́ą́dę́ę́' dahólónígíí inorganic substances bił ááhoo'níiłgo bił hoogáałígíí hináanii nídanidlį́ dóó nahasdzáán bikáá'góó ał'ąą nídadilzhish

translations.definition: The cycle in which nitrogen, carbon, and other inorganic elements of the environment are converted into the organic substances of animals or plants and released back into the environment

entries.sentence: The water cycle is a biogeochemical cycle that describes the way water moves around the Earth.  It includes clouds forming, rain falling, plants absorbing water from the soil, and water evaporating from rivers and lakes.

nahasdzaan-naalkaah.definition: Nahasdzáán ninálkaahgo ha'át'íi da yee hadít'éhígíí, biyi'di dóó bikáá'góó hólónígíí dahazlį́į́'dę́ę́' dóó yee naalnishígíí bíhoo'aah

translations.definition: The science that deals with the earth's physical structure, the materials it is made of, its history, and the processes that act on it.

entries.sentence: A scientist who studies geology can research how structures like mountains and canyons were formed.

nanise-altaasei-naalkaah.definition: Chil dóó nanise' ał'ąą ádaat'éii naalkaah

translations.definition: The scientific study of plants

entries.sentence: You are studying botany when you learn about how plants function, grow, and reproduce.

nanise-bihinaahtsooz-bikagi.definition: Chil nanise', ch'osh doo yit'íinii dóó naaldeehii danilínígíí  yee nanise' dóó nanit'ąígíí bikágí.

translations.definition: A structural layer surrounding cells of plants, bacteria, and other non-animal cells, found just outside the cell membrane

entries.sentence: Penicillin is an antiobitic that can kill bacteria by stopping them from making cell walls.  This is why doctors prescribe it when you have an infection.

nanise-binei-adolzin.definition: Ats'íís bitł'óól yee biinéí dóó hinánígíí yee ádinaalnishígíí

translations.definition: The process through which cells convert sugars into ATP (energy)

entries.sentence: Cellular respiration takes place in the mitochondria and it produces the energy needed for cells to function properly.

nanise-bitoo-dootlizhi.definition: Nanise' yee dadootł'izhígíí adinídíín (shánídíín) łahgo áyiił'įįhgo bee bitoo' naalnish yileehgo biinéí

translations.definition: Any green molecule that converts light energy into chemical energy during photosynthesis

entries.sentence: Plants use different colored pigments, such as chlorophyll, to absorb light and turn it into chemical energy, just as solar panels absorb sunlight and convert it to electricity.

nanise-biziil-iilini.definition: Nanise' bihiináts'ózí biyi'di si'ą́ągo adinídíín yee binéí áyosinígíí

translations.definition: A cell organelle that produces energy through photosynthesis

entries.sentence: If we think of chlorophyll as the solar panels of the plant cell, then chloroplasts are like a field that contains many solar panels.

neli.definition: Na'alkaahgo bééhózin silį́į'ii anáá' bee yitį́įgo haalzíídígíí.

translations.definition: The data measured or seen, often during an experiment

nidaalzheehii-bichiiya.definition: Dahináanii nááná łahdę́ę́' hináanii nida'ałtseedii chodabooł'ínígíí

translations.definition: An organism that is hunted or caught for food, usually by a predator.

entries.sentence: Deer are prey for many animals including mountain lions and people.

nidaiichiih-yeeltaigii.definition: Bíla'ashdla'ii kééhat'į́įgi t'áá hoolzhishgóne' t'áá át'é yéélta'go dóó éí hoolzhishígíí haashį́į́ néelą́ą́' ániid náádaazhchínígíí yéélta'go ałhąą naa'nil.

translations.definition: The number of total live births in comparison to the total population size in a specified community or area over a specified period of time

entries.sentence: If 20 calves are born on a farm with 100 total cows, then we could say that 0.2 calves are born for every cow on the farm (20 calves / 100 cows = 0.2 calves per cow).

nilchi-alaa-anaaniil-lahgo-aaniil.definition: Nahasdzáán bikáá'góó dóó hót'ááhgóó áhoot'éhígíí hado dóó hak'az, níłtsą́, dóó nídaadzasígíí łahgo áániił. T'áá bí éí łahgo ánáánííł nít'ę́ę́' k'adgo níłch'i carbon dioxide wolyéhígíí bilááh ánéelt'e' silį́į́' ałk'idą́ą́' dahináanii bik'éé dahwiilzhiizhii bits'ą́ą́dóó kǫ' choo'į́įgo biniinaa níłch'i ał'ąą ánáá'nííł łahgo áyiilaa.

translations.definition: The change in global or regional weather patterns over time.  This occured naturally over time, but recently increased levels of carbon dioxide due to the use of fossil fuels from the mid to late ą)th century onwards has caused climate change to occur faster.

entries.sentence: Climate change is causing weather extremes, such as increasing temperatures and making droughts more common in the southwest United States.

translations.definition: A chemical element found in the air

entries.sentence: Oxygen is a part of the air that we breathe and the water that we drink.

nilchi-carbon-dioxide-nahoodleel.definition: Níłch'I carbon dioxide wolyéhígíí hót'ááhgóó dóó níłch'I bitahgóó, dahináanii bitahgóó dóó táyi'góó hoo'nááłgo ahééhwiilzhíísh

translations.definition: The movement of carbon dioxide between the atmosphere, living organisms, and land and acquatic ecosystems.

entries.sentence: The carbon cycle describes the way carbon atoms move around the Earth.  Carbon atoms move around when animals decompose, plants grow, fossil fuels are burned, and oceans absorb carbon dioxide (CO2).

nilchi-hado-yilhidideelii.definition: Níłch'i nahasdzáán bikáá'góó náhoniidohígíí yił hidideeł dóó doo yiidichxíid dago biniinaa doo nahasdzáán bits'ą́ąjį' aheldóoh da.

translations.definition: Any gas that can absorb heat from Earth's surface and keeps it from leaving the atmosphere into space.

entries.sentence: Greenhouse gases act like a blanket around Earth, trapping heat instead of letting it escape from the atmosphere.

nilchi-hwiihiila-doo-haahiila.definition: Atsą́ siléí internal organs ła' ats'íís biyi'di dóó ats'íís binaadę́ę́' níłch'I ałnánáálį́įgo yee biinéí dóó bidziilgo ábósin

translations.definition: The group of organs involved in the exchange of gas between an organism and the environment to make energy (respiration).

entries.sentence: In vertebrates, the lungs are an important part of respiratory system as they bring oxygen into the body when we breathe in, and release carbon dioxide when we breathe out.

nilchi-nitrogen-hinila.definition: Níłch'i t'óó ahayóijį' t'áádoole'é dabitah nahasdzáán bikáagi, nahasdzáán biyi'góó, hót'áahdi níłch'i biyi'góó, dóó dahinánígíí bitahgóó ahéénílį́.

translations.definition: The movement of nitrogen through living organisms, the atmosphere, and the ground.

entries.sentence: The order of nucleotides in a strand of DNA and RNA determines the protein produced.

nishigizh.definition: T'áá ha'át'íi da bizhool molecules łą́ hólónídóó áłch'į́į́dí hólǫ́ǫjį' ashiigéeshgo

translations.definition: The movement of molecules from an areas where there are many to where there are fewer

entries.sentence: As you add tea leaves to hot water, the tea will start to spread through the water.  This is an example of diffusion.

oochiil.definition: Bee idí'nóotsąął amá dóó azhé'é bits'ą́ą́dę́ę́' ahidii'ashgo bee t'áá sahdii ázhnoolningo dóó ájít'éego hojileehígíí

translations.definition: The combination of gametes from two individuals to form unique offspring that are genetically different from their parents

entries.sentence: When a male and a female lizard mate, they produce offspring by sexual reproduction.

oochiil-ba-silaii.definition: Ats'íís biyi'di bee idínótsąął dóó adoochííł biniyé dahólónígíí

translations.definition: The group of organs involved in reproduction

entries.sentence: The reason that female animals get pregnant is because their reporductive system has specialized organs for carrying offspring.

translations.definition: The period of time in which an unborn offspring is carried within the body.

entries.sentence: In African elephants, pregnancy can last up to 22 months while pregnancy in cats is about 9 weeks.

sha-biziil-dahinaanii-bitah-siliiigii.definition: Shá bits'ą́ą́dę́ę́' deesdoiígíí hináanii bił nahaz'ą́ądi dóó biyi'di hasht'e dit'eehígíí.

translations.definition: A picture that represents how much energy, initially from the sun, is retained or stored in living organisms at each trophic level in an ecosystem

shanidiin.definition: Shá, jį́honaa'éí, bits'ą́ą́dę́ę́' adinídíín, bits'áníłdoii, dóó bits'áshígishii

translations.definition: Light, heat, and other kinds of energy given off by the Sun

entries.sentence: The function of solar panels are to capture solar energy and convert it to a usable electricity.

taa-lahigi-niighaah.definition: Ha'át'íi da naalnishgo hoolzhish nít'ę́ę́' ni'áániił silį́į́' dóó bits'ą́ą́dóó doo łahgo áhooniił da yileeh éí náasdi hasht'e didooteeł

translations.definition: When the outcome of a process reduces further change and the process becomes stable

entries.sentence: When you get near a hot stove, your skin sends signals to your brain through your nerves to warn you to move away.

translations.definition: A single, separate organism distinguished from others of the same kind

entries.sentence: Type II diabetes is a result of being unable to regulate blood sugar levels because the body doesn't respond to a hormone called insulin correctly.

taadoo-hodinai-daneezna.definition: Hináanii ła' dahyikah nít'ę́ę́' t'áa hodina'í noonééł

translations.definition: Extinction of a large number of species within a short period of time

entries.sentence: Mammals live in a variety of different environments. For example, horses run on land, whales swim in the ocean, and bats fly through the sky.

taadoolee-adinidiin-yee-biziil-anayoodli.definition: Hinááts'ózí cells binaagóó ha'át'íi da doo hináanii bits'ą́ą́dę́ę́' dahólónígíí yee biinéí doo bidziil dooígíí t'áá bí ííł'į́

translations.definition: Cells that create their own energy and biological materials from inorganic chemicals

entries.sentence: Most chemotrophs live in harsh environments like hot spring pools or near thermal vents at the bottom of the ocean.

taadoolee-ateegi-nidaneesaagi-naalkaah.definition: T'áá ha'át'ííshį́į́ baa ajółta' éí doodago jinéł'į́įgo nijiłkaah t'áá nináníłtsojį' éí bee béézhdi'dootį́į́ł biniyé.

translations.definition: To study or examine something in detail, in order to discover more about it

entries.sentence: When you are confused about an object or idea and you want to know how it works, you will analyze it to better understand it.

taadoolee-biziil.definition: T'áádoole'é yee bidziilii yee ałch'į' bótá'ii. Díí ałtsá'niłgo yee bidziilii áyóó' át'éego sidogo bits'áshígish

translations.definition: This type of energy is what holds atoms of a molecule or molecules of a substance together. When these groups of atoms or molecules are separated during a chemical reaction, chemical energy is released, often as heat.  When organisms photosynthesize or digest food, chemical reactions provide chemical energy for their growth and development.

entries.sentence: The battery in your car or phone uses checmical energy from the battery to function.

taadoolee-hooleelgo-lahgo-ahooliil.definition: Ha'átíi da álnéehgo bil hoolzhishgo álnééhígíí náás áániił éí doodago ni'áániił silį́į́'

translations.definition: When the outcome of a process causes further change and the process increases or decreases

entries.sentence: Climate change is accelerating because of a positive feedback loop.  Increased carbon dioxide concentrations cause global temperatures to rise. As oceans warm, they can't absorb as much carbon dioxide, which further warms the climate.

taadoolee-lahgo-aaniil.definition: Ha'át'íi da t'ááła' bizhi'go t'áá náó'nííłgo bits'ą́ą́dóó t'áá ha'át'ííshį́į́ náádleeł doo áko bee ályaii yee da'áhótą'ígíí ałts'á há'nííł éí doodago łahgo át'éego ádaalne'

translations.definition: Any process in which substances combine or are broken down into other substances when chemical bonds between atoms are formed or broken

entries.sentence: Jewlery that is made of nickel and silver can produce a chemical reaction with oxygen that will turn your skin green.

taadoolee-nabihonitaah.definition: Ha'át'íi da t'áá aaníí át'é dóó ó'ooł'įįłgi nabíhonitaahgo naalkaah.

translations.definition: A test done in order to learn something or to discover whether something works or is true.

entries.sentence: If you have dipped your toe into water  to see if it was warm enough for you  to swim, then you have conducted an  experiment!

taalahagi-ahoote.definition: Níláhgóó ał'ąą ánáhoo'níił nidi ats'íístah di doodago hinááhts'óóz/ats'íís bitł'óól biyi'di t'ááłáhági áhoot'é.

translations.definition: The ability of the body or a cell to maintain a stable internal environment despite changes in external conditions.

entries.sentence: You sweat when it is hot and shiver when it is cold because of homeostasis. This is your body's way of staying at a constant temperature.

taalahagi-hazaagi-anooseel.definition: T'ááłáhági haz'ą́ągi bíla'ashdla'ii éí doodago dahináanii t'áá ahedaałt'éhígíí kééhat'į́ dóó da'ahéelchííh

translations.definition: A group of individuals of the same species living and reproducing within a given area

entries.sentence: The people in your town, the pine trees in a forest, and the trout in a lake are examples of populations.

taalahagi-hazaagi-nootiil.definition: Dahináanii kééhat'į́įgi haadahoníłtsogo hahoodzogo bii' haashį́į́ néelt'e' naaldeehígíí.

translations.definition: The number of individuals of the same species living within a given area

entries.sentence: Albuquerque has a higher population density than Window Rock.  About 3,000 people live in each square mile within Albuquerque while Window Rock has 589 people per square mile.

taalahagi-keedahati.definition: T'áá łáhági haz'ą́ągi ha'át'ííshį́į́ dahinááshį́į́ ahił nidaalnish dahinánígíí

translations.definition: Interacting group of various species in a common location

entries.sentence: Hummingbirds and fields of flowers are in the same community because the hummingbirds need the flowers for nectar and the flowers need hummingbirds to spread their pollen.

tatlid.definition: Tátł'id éí táyi' éí doodago hoditłéé'góó dahólǫ́ bits'íís bitł'óól t'áá łá'í éí doodago łą́ áádóó dootł'izh łeh éí doodago t'áá łahgo ánoolnin łeh.
tatlid.literal: łʼid= smoke , tá= under. =under water smoke type of thing/describing the hazyness of how it looks.

translations.definition: Unicellular or multicellular organisms occurring in fresh or salt water or moist ground, that have chlorophyll and other pigments but lack true stems, roots, and leaves

entries.sentence: Algae can grow as single cells or large green clumps and sometimes appear to cover the entire water surface if enough of them grow. They are similar to plants because they also use the sun to make their own food.

to-binahata.definition: Tó nahasdzáán bikáá'góó dóó biyi' hólǫ́  dóó hót'ááhgóó dahólǫ́ éí hoo'nááł dóó danílínígíí bił ahééhwiizhíísh

translations.definition: The movement of water on, above, or below the surface of our planet.

entries.sentence: The water cycle describes how water moves around the earth.  Water moves around when it rains, when rivers transport it long distances, when it evaporates from the surface of leaves, or when ocean currents move it around the globe.

to-bitah-yileehii.definition: Ha'át'íi da bizhool,  molecules, éí doodago bikáá'góó tó bił ałtah yileehígíí

translations.definition: Molecules and surfaces that do interact with water

entries.sentence: Salt is hydrophilic; it is easily dissolved when it is mixed with water.

to-doo-bitah-yileehii.definition: Ha'át'íi da bizhool,  molecules, éí doodago bikáá'góó tó doo bił ałtah yileehígíí

translations.definition: Molecules and surfaces that do no interact with water

entries.sentence: Oil is hydrophobic; it will not dissolve when mixed with water.

yaa.definition: T'áádoole'é hináago nááná łahjį' hináanii choyooł'į́įgo yee hinánígíí.

translations.definition: An organism that lives on or in another organism (its host).

entries.sentence: You could get a parasite like a tapeworm in your gut when you eat undercooked meat from infected animals.

yaakehgo-hina.definition: Hináanii nááná ła' choo'į́įgo bee jiináago.

translations.definition: The practice of living as a parasite in or on another organism

entries.sentence: After they hatch from eggs, ticks survive through parasitsm where they eat blood from mammals.

yah-iiineeh.definition: Bíla'ashdla'ii tádínéehgo bíla'ashdla'ii bił haz'ą́ągo yitah yinééh éí doodago nááná łahgi haz'ą́ągi niinééhgo

translations.definition: The migration of individuals into a particular population or area from other populations or areas.  Compare with emigration.

entries.sentence: When you get a cut it is important to wash it with soap and water so you do not get an infection.

yizhi-doo-oolyehigii.definition: Na'alkaahk'ehgo t'áá ałtsoní bízhi' dóó íiyisíí át'éhígíí baa hane'

translations.definition: The science of naming and describing organisms.

entries.sentence: A wolf pack has a large territory of up to 1000 square miles that it defends from other wolves.  If let outside, a house cat will defend a small territory of less than 10 square miles and will kill both pests and beneficial wildlife.
